Do not open the pool during startup.\n222:                 configure=self._configure_connection,\n223:                 check=ConnectionPool.check_connection if enable_checks else None,\n224:                 **pool_options,\n225:             )\n226:             # setdefault() ensures that multiple threads don't set this in\n227:             # parallel. Since we do not open the pool during it's init above,\n228:             # this means that at worst during startup multiple threads generate\n229:             # pool objects and the first to set it wins.\n230:             self._connection_pools.setdefault(self.alias, pool)\n231: \n232:         return self._connection_pools[self.alias]\n233: \n234:     def close_pool(self):\n235:         if self.pool:\n236:             self.pool.close()\n237:             del self._connection_pools[self.alias]\n238: \n239:     def get_database_version(self):\n240:         \"\"\"\n241:         Return a tuple of the database's version.\n242:         E.g. for pg_version 120004, return (12, 4).\n243:         \"\"\"\n244:         return divmod(self.pg_version, 10000)\n245: \n246:     def get_connection_params(self):\n247:         settings_dict = self.settings_dict\n248:         # None may be used to connect to the default 'postgres' db\n249:         if settings_dict[\"NAME\"] == \"\" and not settings_dict[\"OPTIONS\"].get(\"service\"):\n250:             raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n251: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"618:     # ##### Thread safety handling #####\\n619: \\n620:     @property\\n621:     def allow_thread_sharing(self):\\n622:         with self._thread_sharing_lock:\\n623:             return self._thread_sharing_count > 0\\n624: \\n625:     def inc_thread_sharing(self):\\n626:         with self._thread_sharing_lock:\\n627:             self._thread_sharing_count += 1\\n628: \\n629:     def dec_thread_sharing(self):\\n630:         with self._thread_sharing_lock:\\n631:             if self._thread_sharing_count <= 0:\\n632:                 raise RuntimeError(\\n633:                     \\\"Cannot decrement the thread sharing count below zero.\\\"\\n634:                 )\\n635:             self._thread_sharing_count -= 1\\n636: \\n637:     def validate_thread_sharing(self):\\n638:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n639:         Validate that the connection isn't accessed by another thread than the\\n640:         one which originally created it, unless the connection was explicitly\\n641:         authorized to be shared between threads (via the `inc_thread_sharing()`\\n642:         method). Raise an exception if the validation fails.\\n643:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n644:         if not (self.allow_thread_sharing or self._thread_ident == _thread.get_ident()):\\n645:             raise DatabaseError(\\n646:                 \\\"DatabaseWrapper objects created in a \\\"\\n647:                 \\\"thread can only be used in that same thread. wrap_database_errors(self):\\n663:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n664:         Context manager and decorator that re-throws backend-specific database\\n665:         exceptions using Django's common wrappers.\\n666:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n667:         return DatabaseErrorWrapper(self)\\n668: \\n669:     def chunked_cursor(self):\\n670:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n671:         Return a cursor that    def make_debug_cursor(self, cursor):\\n677:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Create a cursor that logs all queries in self.queries_log.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n678:         return utils.CursorDebugWrapper(cursor, \\n684:     @contextmanager\\n685:     def temporary_connection(self):\\n686:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n687:         Context manager that ensures that a connection is established, and\\n688:         if it opened one, closes it to avoid leaving a dangling connection.\\n689:         This is useful for operations outside of the request-response cycle.\\n690: \\n691:         Provide a cursor: with self.temporary_connection() as cursor: ...\\n692:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n693:         must_close = self.connection is None\\n694:         try:\\n695:             with self.cursor() as cursor:\\n696:                 yield cursor\\n697:         finally:\\n698:             if must_close:\\n699:                 self.close()\\n700: \\n701:     @contextmanager\\n702:     def _nodb_cursor(self):\\n703:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n704:         Return a cursor from an alternative connection to be used when there is\\n705:         no need to access the main database, specifically for test db\\n706:         creation/deletion. This also prevents the production database from\\n707:         being exposed to potential child threads while (or after) the test\\n708:         database is destroyed. \\n255:     def close(self) -> None:\\n256:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n257:         Clean up all resources used by the DefaultStager. Shuts down the ThreadPoolExecutor\\n258:         used for async staging operations and cleans up the underlying StateDictStager's\\n259:         cached storages. Should be called when the stager is no longer needed to prevent\\n260:         resource leaks, especially in long-running applications. After calling close(),\\n261:         the stager should not be used for further staging operations.\\n262: \\n263:         Example Usage:\\n264:             stager = DefaultStager(StagingOptions(use_async_staging=True))\\n265:             future = stager.stage(state_dict)\\n266:             result = future.result()\\n267:             stager.close()  # Clean up all resources\\n268:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n269: \",\"endLine\":269,\"startLine\":254},{\"content\":\"98: \\n99:     def close(self) -> None:\\n100:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n101:         Clean up all resources used by the stager.\\n102:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n103: \",\"endLine\":103,\"startLine\":98},{\"content\":\"473: \\n474:     def close(self) -> None:\\n475:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n476:         Clean up resources. Persisted files are intentionally left for future discovery.\\n477:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n478: \",\"endLine\":478,\"startLine\":473}],\"file\":\"torch/distributed/checkpoint/staging.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/pytorch/pytorch\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch/-/blob/torch/distributed/checkpoint/staging.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"683:             "content": "355:    * Creates an Error with context-specific remediation based on the actual\n356:    * error message and the current sessionMode.\n357:    */\n358:   private createConnectionError(message: string, sessionMode: string): Error {\n359:     const lowerMessage = message.toLowerCase();\n360: \n361:     // \"already running for the current profile\" \u2014 persistent mode profile lock\n362:     if (lowerMessage.includes('already running')) {\n363:       if (sessionMode === 'persistent' || sessionMode === 'isolated') {\n364:         return new Error(\n365:           `Could not connect to Chrome: ${message}\\n\\n` +\n366:             `The Chrome profile is locked by another running instance.\\n` +\n367: ",
            "endLine": 367,
            "startLine": 355
          },
          {
            "content": "184:   /**\n185:    * Closes browser and cleans up connections.\n186:    * The browser process is managed by chrome-devtools-mcp, so closing\n187:    * the transport will terminate the browser.\n188: ",
            "endLine": 188,
            "startLine": 184
          },
          {
            "content": "374:       return new Error(\n375:         `Could not connect to Chrome: ${message}\\n\\n` +\n376:           `The Chrome profile is locked.\\n` +\n377:           `Close other Chrome instances and try again.`,\n378:       );\n379: ",

            "endLine": 57,
            "startLine": 48

            "content": "90: \n91:     def test_closes_connections(self):\n92:         # The server's request thread sets this event after closing\n93:         # its database connections.\n94:         closed_event = self.server_thread.httpd._connections_closed\n95:         conn = self.conn\n96:         # Open a connection to the database.\n97:         conn.connect()\n98:         self.assertIsNotNone(conn.connection)\n99:         with self.urlopen(\"/model_view/\") as f:\n100: ",

            "content": "254: \n255:     def close(self) -> None:\n256:         \"\"\"\n257:         Clean up all resources used by the DefaultStager. Shuts down the ThreadPoolExecutor\n258:         used for async staging operations and cleans up the underlying StateDictStager's\n259:         cached storages. Should be called when the stager is no longer needed to prevent\n260:         resource leaks, especially in long-running applications. After calling close(),\n261:         the stager should not be used for further staging operations.\n262: \n263:         Example Usage:\n264:             stager = DefaultStager(StagingOptions(use_async_staging=True))\n265:             future = stager.stage(state_dict)\n266:             result = future.result()\n267:             stager.close()  # Clean up all resources\n268:         \"\"\"\n269: ",
            "endLine": 269,
            "startLine": 254
          },
          {
            "content": "98: \n99:     def close(self) -> None:\n100:         \"\"\"\n101:         Clean up all resources used by the stager.\n102:         \"\"\"\n103: ",

          },
          {
            "content": "473: \n474:     def close(self) -> None:\n475:         \"\"\"\n476:         Clean up resources. Persisted files are intentionally left for future discovery.\n477:         \"\"\"\n478: ",

          }

            "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Checkpoint writer functionality for machine learning models.\n3: \n4: This module provides classes for writing checkpoints to storage, including\n5: determining checkpoint layout, configuring the writer, and defining hooks\n6: for custom actions during the checkpoint writing process.\n7: \"\"\"\n8: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"380: \\n381:         # Commit after setting the time zone.\\n382:         commit_tz = self._configure_timezone(connection)\\n383:         # Set the role on the connection. This is useful if the credential used\\n384:         # to login is not the same as the role that owns database resources. As\\n385:         # can be the case when using temporary or ephemeral credentials.\\n386:         commit_role = self._configure_role(connection)\\n387: \\n388:         return commit_role or commit_tz\\n389: \\n390:     def _close(self):\\n391:         if self.connection is not None:\\n392:             # `wrap_database_errors` only works for `putconn` as long as there\\n393:             # is no `reset` function set in the pool because it is deferred\\n394:             # into a thread and not directly executed.\\n395:             with self.wrap_database_errors:\\n396:                 if self.pool:\\n397:                     # Ensure the correct pool is returned. This is a workaround\\n398:                     # for tests so a pool can be changed on setting changes\\n399:                     # (e.g. USE_TZ, TIME_ZONE).\\n400:                     self.connection._pool.putconn(self.connection)\\n401:                     # Connection can no longer be used.\\n402:                     self.connection = None\\n403:                 else:\\n404:                     return self.connection.close()\\n405: \\n406:     def init_connection_state(self):\\n407:         super().init_connection_state()\\n408: \\n409:         if self.connection is not None and not self.pool:\\n410:             commit = self._configure_connection(self.connection)\\n411: \\n412:             if commit and not self.get_autocommit():\\n413:                 self.connection.commit()\\n414: \\n415:     @async_unsafe\\n416:     def create_cursor(self, name=None):\\n417:         if name:\\n418:             if is_psycopg3 and (\\n419:                 self.settings_dict[\\\"OPTIONS\\\"].get(\\\"server_side_binding\\\") is not True\\n420:             ):\\n421:                 # psycopg >= 3 forces the usage of server-side bindings for\\n422:                 # named cursors so a specialized class that implements\\n423:                 # server-side cursors while performing client-side bindings\\n424:                 # must be used if `server_side_binding` is disabled (default).\\n425:                 cursor = ServerSideCursor(\\n426:                     self.connection,\\n427:                     name=name,\\n428:                     scrollable=False,\\n429:                     withhold=self.connection.autocommit,\\n430:                 )\\n431: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/postgresql/base.py%3FL380-430\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "380: \n381:         # Commit after setting the time zone.\n382:         commit_tz = self._configure_timezone(connection)\n383:         # Set the role on the connection. This is useful if the credential used\n384:         # to login is not the same as the role that owns database resources. As\n385:         # can be the case when using temporary or ephemeral credentials.\n386:         commit_role = self._configure_role(connection)\n387: \n388:         return commit_role or commit_tz\n389: \n390:     def _close(self):\n391:         if self.connection is not None:\n392:             # `wrap_database_errors` only works for `putconn` as long as there\n393:             # is no `reset` function set in the pool because it is deferred\n394:             # into a thread and not directly executed.\n395:             with self.wrap_database_errors:\n396:                 if self.pool:\n397:                     # Ensure the correct pool is returned. This is a workaround\n398:                     # for tests so a pool can be changed on setting changes\n399:                     # (e.g. USE_TZ, TIME_ZONE).\n400:                     self.connection._pool.putconn(self.connection)\n401:                     # Connection can no longer be used.\n402:                     self.connection = None\n403:                 else:\n404:                     return self.connection.close()\n405: \n406:     def init_connection_state(self):\n407:         super().init_connection_state()\n408: \n409:         if self.connection is not None and not self.pool:\n410:             commit = self._configure_connection(self.connection)\n411: \n412:             if commit and not self.get_autocommit():\n413:                 self.connection.commit()\n414: \n415:     @async_unsafe\n416:     def create_cursor(self, name=None):\n417:         if name:\n418:             if is_psycopg3 and (\n419:                 self.settings_dict[\"OPTIONS\"].get(\"server_side_binding\") is not True\n420:             ):\n421:                 # psycopg >= 3 forces the usage of server-side bindings for\n422:                 # named cursors so a specialized class that implements\n423:                 # server-side cursors while performing client-side bindings\n424:                 # must be used if `server_side_binding` is disabled (default).\n425:                 cursor = ServerSideCursor(\n426:                     self.connection,\n427:                     name=name,\n428:                     scrollable=False,\n429:                     withhold=self.connection.autocommit,\n430:                 )\n431: ",

Register the cursor timezone only if the connection disagrees, to\\n442:             # avoid copying the adapter map.\\n443:             tzloader = self.connection.adapters.get_loader(TIMESTAMPTZ_OID, Format.TEXT)\\n444:             if self.timezone != tzloader.timezone:\\n445:                 register_tzloader(self.timezone, cursor)\\n446:         else:\\n447:             cursor.tzinfo_factory = self.tzinfo_factory if settings.USE_TZ else None\\n448:         return cursor\\n449: \\n450:     def tzinfo_factory(self, offset):\\n451:         return self.timezone\\n452: \\n453:     @async_unsafe\\n454:     def chunked_cursor(self):\\n455:         self._named_cursor_idx += 1\\n456:         # Get the current async task\\n457:         # Note that right now this is behind @async_unsafe, so this is\\n458:         # unreachable, but in future we'll start loosening this restriction.\\n459:         # For now, it's here so that every use of \\\"threading\\\" is\\n460:         # also async-compatible.\\n461:         try:\\n462:             current_task = asyncio.current_task()\\n463:         except RuntimeError:\\n464:             current_task = None\\n465:         # Use that and the thread ident to get a unique name\\n471:         return self._cursor(\\n472:             name=\\\"_django_curs_%d_%s_%d\\\"\\n473:             % (\\n474:                 # Avoid reusing name in other threads / tasks\\n475:                 threading.current_thread().ident,\\n476:                 task_ident,\\n477:                 self._named_cursor_idx,\\n478:             )\\n479:         )\\n480: \\n481:     def _set_autocommit(self, autocommit):\\n482:         with self.wrap_database_errors:\\n483:             self.connection.autocommit = autocommit\\n484: \\n485:     def check_constraints(self, table_names=None):\\n486:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n487:         Check constraints by setting them to immediate. Use a psycopg cursor directly, bypassing Django's utilities.\\n499:             with self.connection.cursor() as cursor:\\n500:                 cursor.execute(\\\"SELECT 1\\\")\\n501:         except Database.Error:\\n502:             return False\\n503:         else:\\n504:             return True\\n505: \\n506:     def close_if_health_check_failed(self):\\n507:         if self.pool:\\n508:             # The pool only returns healthy connections.\\n509:             return\\n510:         return super().close_if_health_check_failed()\\n511: \\n512:     @contextmanager\\n513:     def _nodb_cursor(self):\\n514:         cursor = None\\n515:         try:\\n516:             with super()._nodb_cursor() as cursor:\\n517:                 yield cursor\\n518:         except (Database.DatabaseError, WrappedDatabaseError):\\n519:             if cursor is not None:\\n520:                 raise\\n521:             warnings.warn(\\n522:                 \\\"Normally Django will use a connection to the 'postgres' database \\\"\\n523:                 \\\"to avoid running initialization queries against the production \\\"\\n524:                 \\\"database when it's not needed (for example, when running tests). \\\"\\n525:                 \\\"Django was unable to create a connection to the 'postgres' database \\\"\\n526:                 \\\"and will use the first PostgreSQL database instead.\\\",\\n527:                 RuntimeWarning,\\n528:             )\\n529:             for connection in connections.all():\\n530:                 if (\\n531: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/postgresql/base.py%3FL430-530\"}",

{
  "content": "{\"base\":\"HEAD~20\",\"endCursor\":\"5\",\"files\":[{\"added\":1,\"deleted\":1,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" \\n #changelist .changelist-form-container {\\n     display: flex;\\n-    flex-wrap: wrap;\\n     align-items: flex-start;\\n     width: 100%;\\n }\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":2},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":2}},{\"body\":\" }\\n \\n #changelist .changelist-form-container:has(#changelist-filter) > div {\\n+    min-width: 0;\\n     max-width: calc(100% - 270px);\\n }\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":15},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":16}}],\"path\":\"django/contrib/admin/static/admin/css/changelists.css\"},{\"added\":2,\"deleted\":1,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"         print(\\n             f\\\"{now}\\\\n\\\"\\n             f\\\"Django version {version}, using settings {settings.SETTINGS_MODULE!r}\\\\n\\\"\\n-            f\\\"Starting development server at {self.protocol}://{addr}:{server_port}/\\\\n\\\"\\n+            f\\\"Starting WSGI development server at {self.protocol}://{addr}\\\"\\n+            f\\\":{server_port}/\\\\n\\\"\\n             f\\\"Quit the server with {quit_command}.\\\",\\n             file=self.stdout,\\n         )\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":184},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":184},\"section\":\"class Command(BaseCommand):\"}],\"path\":\"django/core/management/commands/runserver.py\"},{\"added\":8,\"deleted\":3,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"                 value = parse_time(value)\\n         return value\\n \\n+    @staticmethod\\n+    def _create_decimal(value):\\n+        if isinstance(value, (int, str)):\\n+            return decimal.Decimal(value)\\n+        return decimal.Context(prec=15).create_decimal_from_float(value)\\n+\\n     def get_decimalfield_converter(self, expression):\\n         # SQLite stores only 15 significant digits. Digits coming from\\n         # float inaccuracy must be removed.\\n-        create_decimal = decimal.Context(prec=15).create_decimal_from_float\\n         if isinstance(expression, Col):\\n             quantize_value = decimal.Decimal(1).scaleb(\\n                 -expression.output_field.decimal_places\\n             )\\n \\n             def converter(value, expression, connection):\\n                 if value is not None:\\n-                    return create_decimal(value).quantize(\\n+                    return self._create_decimal(value).quantize(\\n                         quantize_value, Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"591:     def close_if_unusable_or_obsolete(self):\\n592:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n593:         Close the current connection if unrecoverable errors have occurred\\n594:         or if it outlived its maximum age.\\n595:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n596:         if self.connection is not None:\\n597:             self.health_check_done = False\\n598:             # If the application didn't restore the original autocommit\\n599:             # setting, don't take chances, drop the connection.\\n600:             if self.get_autocommit() != self.settings_dict[\\\"AUTOCOMMIT\\\"]:\\n601:                 self.close()\\n602:                 return\\n603: \\n604:             # If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\\n605:             # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\\n606:             if self.errors_occurred:\\n607:                 if self.is_usable():\\n608:                     self.errors_occurred = False\\n609:                     self.health_check_done = True\\n610:                 else:\\n611:                     self.close()\\n612:                     return\\n613: \\n614:             if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= self.close_at:\\n615:                 self.close()\\n616:                 return\\n617: \\n618:     # ##### Thread safety handling #####\\n619: \\n620:     @property\\n621: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/django/django@e8ab2bb83fc6d3c0f5d998d1a41ebaebacaa1a28/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL591-620\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "591:     def close_if_unusable_or_obsolete(self):\n592:         \"\"\"\n593:         Close the current connection if unrecoverable errors have occurred\n594:         or if it outlived its maximum age.\n595:         \"\"\"\n596:         if self.connection is not None:\n597:             self.health_check_done = False\n598:             # If the application didn't restore the original autocommit\n599:             # setting, don't take chances, drop the connection.\n600:             if self.get_autocommit() != self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"]:\n601:                 self.close()\n602:                 return\n603: \n604:             # If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\n605:             # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\n606:             if self.errors_occurred:\n607:                 if self.is_usable():\n608:                     self.errors_occurred = False\n609:                     self.health_check_done = True\n610:                 else:\n611:                     self.close()\n612:                     return\n613: \n614:             if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= self.close_at:\n615:                 self.close()\n616:                 return\n617: \n618:     # ##### Thread safety handling #####\n619: \n620:     @property\n621: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/django/django@e8ab2bb83fc6d3c0f5d998d1a41ebaebacaa1a28/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL591-620"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"236:     @async_unsafe\\n237:     def connect(self):\\n238:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Connect to the database. Assume that the connection is closed.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n239:         # Check for invalid configurations.\\n240:         self.check_settings()\\n241:         # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\\n242:         self.in_atomic_block = False\\n243:         self.savepoint_ids = []\\n244:         self.atomic_blocks = []\\n245:         self.needs_rollback = False\\n246:         # Reset parameters defining when to close/health-check the connection.\\n247:         self.health_check_enabled = self.settings_dict[\\\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\\\"]\\n248:         max_age = self.settings_dict[\\\"CONN_MAX_AGE\\\"]\\n249:         self.close_at = None if max_age is None else time.monotonic() + max_age\\n250:         self.closed_in_transaction = False\\n251:         self.errors_occurred = False\\n252:         # New connections are healthy.\\n253:         self.health_check_done = True\\n254:         # Establish the connection\\n255:         conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\\n256:         self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\\n257:         self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\\\"AUTOCOMMIT\\\"])\\n258:         self.init_connection_state()\\n259:         connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\\n260: \\n261: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/django/django@e8ab2bb83fc6d3c0f5d998d1a41ebaebacaa1a28/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL236-260\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "236:     @async_unsafe\n237:     def connect(self):\n238:         \"\"\"Connect to the database. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n239:         # Check for invalid configurations.\n240:         self.check_settings()\n241:         # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n242:         self.in_atomic_block = False\n243:         self.savepoint_ids = []\n244:         self.atomic_blocks = []\n245:         self.needs_rollback = False\n246:         # Reset parameters defining when to close/health-check the connection.\n247:         self.health_check_enabled = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\"]\n248:         max_age = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_MAX_AGE\"]\n249:         self.close_at = None if max_age is None else time.monotonic() + max_age\n250:         self.closed_in_transaction = False\n251:         self.errors_occurred = False\n252:         # New connections are healthy.\n253:         self.health_check_done = True\n254:         # Establish the connection\n255:         conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\n256:         self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\n257:         self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"])\n258:         self.init_connection_state()\n259:         connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\n260: \n261: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/django/django@e8ab2bb83fc6d3c0f5d998d1a41ebaebacaa1a28/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL236-260"
  }

Prefer connections['default'] instead.\\n43: connection = ConnectionProxy(connections, DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS)\\n44: \\n45: \\n46: # Register an event to reset saved queries when a Django request is started.\\n47: def reset_queries(**kwargs):\\n48:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\\n49:         conn.queries_log.clear()\\n50: \\n51: \\n52: signals.request_started.connect(reset_queries)\\n53: \\n54: \\n55: # Register an event to reset transaction state and close connections past\\n56: # their lifetime.\\n57: def close_old_connections(**kwargs):\\n58:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\\n59:         conn.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n60: \\n61: \\n62: signals.request_started.connect(close_old_connections)\\n63: signals.request_finished.connect(close_old_connections)\\n64: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/django/django@e8ab2bb83fc6d3c0f5d998d1a41ebaebacaa1a28/-/blob/django/db/__init__.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: from django.core import signals\n2: from django.db.utils import (\n3:     DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS,\n4:     DJANGO_VERSION_PICKLE_KEY,\n5:     ConnectionHandler,\n6:     ConnectionRouter,\n7:     DatabaseError,\n8:     DataError,\n9:     Error,\n10:     IntegrityError,\n11:     InterfaceError,\n12:     InternalError,\n13:     NotSupportedError,\n14:     OperationalError,\n15:     ProgrammingError,\n16: )\n17: from django.utils.connection import ConnectionProxy\n18: \n19: __all__ = [\n20:     \"close_old_connections\",\n21:     \"connection\",\n22:     \"connections\",\n23:     \"reset_queries\",\n24:     \"router\",\n25:     \"DatabaseError\",\n26:     \"IntegrityError\",\n27:     \"InternalError\",\n28:     \"ProgrammingError\",\n29:     \"DataError\",\n30:     \"NotSupportedError\",\n31:     \"Error\",\n32:     \"InterfaceError\",\n33:     \"OperationalError\",\n34:     \"DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS\",\n35:     \"DJANGO_VERSION_PICKLE_KEY\",\n36: ]\n37: \n38: connections = ConnectionHandler()\n39: \n40: router = ConnectionRouter()\n41: \n42: # For backwards compatibility. Prefer connections['default'] instead.\n43: connection = ConnectionProxy(connections, DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS)\n44: \n45: \n46: # Register an event to reset saved queries when a Django request is started.\n47: def reset_queries(**kwargs):\n48:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\n49:         conn.queries_log.clear()\n50: \n51: \n52: signals.request_started.connect(reset_queries)\n53: \n54: \n55: # Register an event to reset transaction state and close connections past\n56: # their lifetime.\n57: def close_old_connections(**kwargs):\n58:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\n59:         conn.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n60: \n61: \n62: signals.request_started.connect(close_old_connections)\n63: signals.request_finished.connect(close_old_connections)\n64: ",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"780:             transaction.set_rollback(True)\\n781: \\n782: \\n783: class ThreadTests(TransactionTestCase):\\n784:     available_apps = [\\\"backends\\\"]\\n785: \\n786:     def test_default_connection_thread_local(self):\\n787:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n788:         The default connection (i.e. django.db.connection) is different for\\n789:         each thread (#17258).\\n790:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n791:         # Map connections by id because connections with identical aliases\\n792:         # have the same hash.\\n793:         connections_dict = {}\\n794:         with connection.cursor():\\n795:             pass\\n796:         connections_dict[id(connection)] = connection\\n797: \\n798:         def runner():\\n799:             # Passing django.db.connection between threads doesn't work while\\n800:             # connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] does.\\n801:             from django.db import connections\\n802: \\n803:             connection = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\\n804:             # Allow thread sharing so the connection can be closed by the\\n805:             # main thread.\\n806:             connection.inc_thread_sharing()\\n807:             with connection.cursor():\\n808:                 pass\\n809:             connections_dict[id(connection)] = connection\\n810: \\n811:         try:\\n812:             for x in range(2):\\n813:                 t = threading.Thread(target=runner)\\n814:                 t.start()\\n815:                 t.join()\\n816:             # Each created connection got different inner connection.\\n817:             self.assertEqual(\\n818:                 len({conn.connection for conn in connections_dict.values()}), 3\\n819:             )\\n820:         finally:\\n821:             # Finish by closing the connections opened by the other threads\\n822:             # (the connection opened in the main thread will automatically be\\n823:             # closed on teardown).\\n824:             for conn in connections_dict.values():\\n825:                 if conn is not connection and conn.allow_thread_sharing:\\n826:                     conn.validate_thread_sharing()\\n827:                     conn._close()\\n828:                     conn.dec_thread_sharing()\\n829: \\n830:     def test_connections_thread_local(self):\\n831:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n832:         The connections are different for each thread (#17258).\\n833:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n834:         # Map connections by id because connections with identical aliases\\n835:         # have the same hash.\\n836:         connections_dict = {}\\n837:         for conn in connections.all():\\n838:             connections_dict[id(conn)] = conn\\n839: \\n840:         def runner():\\n841:             from django.db import connections\\n842: \\n843:             for conn in connections.all():\\n844:                 # Allow thread sharing so the connection can be closed by the\\n845:                 # main thread.\\n846:                 conn.inc_thread_sharing()\\n847:                 connections_dict[id(conn)] = conn\\n848: \\n849:         try:\\n850:             num_new_threads = 2\\n851:             for x in range(num_new_threads):\\n852:                 t = threading.Thread(target=runner)\\n853:                 t.start()\\n854:                 t.join()\\n855:             self.assertEqual(\\n856:                 len(connections_dict),\\n857:                 len(connections.all()) * (num_new_threads + 1),\\n858:             )\\n859:         finally:\\n860:             # Finish by closing the connections opened by the other threads\\n861:             # (the connection opened in the main thread will automatically be\\n862:             # closed on teardown).\\n863:             for conn in connections_dict.values():\\n864:                 if conn is not connection and conn.allow_thread_sharing:\\n865:                     conn.close()\\n866:                     conn.dec_thread_sharing()\\n867: \\n868:     def test_pass_connection_between_threads(self):\\n869:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n870:         A connection can be passed from one thread to the other (#17258).\\n871: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/tests/backends/tests.py%3FL780-870\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "780:             transaction.set_rollback(True)\n781: \n782: \n783: class ThreadTests(TransactionTestCase):\n784:     available_apps = [\"backends\"]\n785: \n786:     def test_default_connection_thread_local(self):\n787:         \"\"\"\n788:         The default connection (i.e. django.db.connection) is different for\n789:         each thread (#17258).\n790:         \"\"\"\n791:         # Map connections by id because connections with identical aliases\n792:         # have the same hash.\n793:         connections_dict = {}\n794:         with connection.cursor():\n795:             pass\n796:         connections_dict[id(connection)] = connection\n797: \n798:         def runner():\n799:             # Passing django.db.connection between threads doesn't work while\n800:             # connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] does.\n801:             from django.db import connections\n802: \n803:             connection = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\n804:             # Allow thread sharing so the connection can be closed by the\n805:             # main thread.\n806:             connection.inc_thread_sharing()\n807:             with connection.cursor():\n808:                 pass\n809:             connections_dict[id(connection)] = connection\n810: \n811:         try:\n812:             for x in range(2):\n813:                 t = threading.Thread(target=runner)\n814:                 t.start()\n815:                 t.join()\n816:             # Each created connection got different inner connection.\n817:             self.assertEqual(\n818:                 len({conn.connection for conn in connections_dict.values()}), 3\n819:             )\n820:         finally:\n821:             # Finish by closing the connections opened by the other threads\n822:             # (the connection opened in the main thread will automatically be\n823:             # closed on teardown).\n824:             for conn in connections_dict.values():\n825:                 if conn is not connection and conn.allow_thread_sharing:\n826:                     conn.validate_thread_sharing()\n827:                     conn._close()\n828:                     conn.dec_thread_sharing()\n829: \n830:     def test_connections_thread_local(self):\n831:         \"\"\"\n832:         The connections are different for each thread (#17258).\n833:         \"\"\"\n834:         # Map connections by id because connections with identical aliases\n835:         # have the same hash.\n836:         connections_dict = {}\n837:         for conn in connections.all():\n838:             connections_dict[id(conn)] = conn\n839: \n840:         def runner():\n841:             from django.db import connections\n842: \n843:             for conn in connections.all():\n844:                 # Allow thread sharing so the connection can be closed by the\n845:                 # main thread.\n846:                 conn.inc_thread_sharing()\n847:                 connections_dict[id(conn)] = conn\n848: \n849:         try:\n850:             num_new_threads = 2\n851:             for x in range(num_new_threads):\n852:                 t = threading.Thread(target=runner)\n853:                 t.start()\n854:                 t.join()\n855:             self.assertEqual(\n856:                 len(connections_dict),\n857:                 len(connections.all()) * (num_new_threads + 1),\n858:             )\n859:         finally:\n860:             # Finish by closing the connections opened by the other threads\n861:             # (the connection opened in the main thread will automatically be\n862:             # closed on teardown).\n863:             for conn in connections_dict.values():\n864:                 if conn is not connection and conn.allow_thread_sharing:\n865:                     conn.close()\n866:                     conn.dec_thread_sharing()\n867: \n868:     def test_pass_connection_between_threads(self):\n869:         \"\"\"\n870:         A connection can be passed from one thread to the other (#17258).\n871: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/tests/backends/tests.py%3FL780-870"

It is no longer used within\\n191:         # Django itself.\\n192:         return self.settings\\n193: \\n194:     def create_connection(self, alias):\\n195:         db = self.settings[alias]\\n196:         backend = load_backend(db[\\\"ENGINE\\\"])\\n197:         return backend.DatabaseWrapper(db, alias)\\n198: \\n199: \\n200: class ConnectionRouter:\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/utils.py%3FL150-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "150:     def configure_settings(self, databases):\n151:         databases = super().configure_settings(databases)\n152:         if databases == {}:\n153:             databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] = {\"ENGINE\": \"django.db.backends.dummy\"}\n154:         elif DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS not in databases:\n155:             raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n156:                 f\"You must define a '{DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}' database.\"\n157:             )\n158:         elif databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] == {}:\n159:             databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS][\"ENGINE\"] = \"django.db.backends.dummy\"\n160: \n161:         # Configure default settings.\n162:         for conn in databases.values():\n163:             conn.setdefault(\"ATOMIC_REQUESTS\", False)\n164:             conn.setdefault(\"AUTOCOMMIT\", True)\n165:             conn.setdefault(\"ENGINE\", \"django.db.backends.dummy\")\n166:             if conn[\"ENGINE\"] == \"django.db.backends.\" or not conn[\"ENGINE\"]:\n167:                 conn[\"ENGINE\"] = \"django.db.backends.dummy\"\n168:             conn.setdefault(\"CONN_MAX_AGE\", 0)\n169:             conn.setdefault(\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\", False)\n170:             conn.setdefault(\"OPTIONS\", {})\n171:             conn.setdefault(\"TIME_ZONE\", None)\n172:             for setting in [\"NAME\", \"USER\", \"PASSWORD\", \"HOST\", \"PORT\"]:\n173:                 conn.setdefault(setting, \"\")\n174: \n175:             test_settings = conn.setdefault(\"TEST\", {})\n176:             default_test_settings = [\n177:                 (\"CHARSET\", None),\n178:                 (\"COLLATION\", None),\n179:                 (\"MIGRATE\", True),\n180:                 (\"MIRROR\", None),\n181:                 (\"NAME\", None),\n182:             ]\n183:             for key, value in default_test_settings:\n184:                 test_settings.setdefault(key, value)\n185:         return databases\n186: \n187:     @property\n188:     def databases(self):\n189:         # Maintained for backward compatibility as some 3rd party packages have\n190:         # made use of this private API in the past. It is no longer used within\n191:         # Django itself.\n192:         return self.settings\n193: \n194:     def create_connection(self, alias):\n195:         db = self.settings[alias]\n196:         backend = load_backend(db[\"ENGINE\"])\n197:         return backend.DatabaseWrapper(db, alias)\n198: \n199: \n200: class ConnectionRouter:\n201: ",

    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: SQLite backend for the sqlite3 module in the standard library.\n3: \"\"\"\n4: \n5: import datetime\n6: import decimal\n7: import warnings\n8: from collections.abc import Mapping\n9: from itertools import chain, tee\n10: from sqlite3 import dbapi2 as Database\n11: \n12: from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured\n13: from django.db import IntegrityError\n14: from django.db.backends.base.base import BaseDatabaseWrapper\n15: from django.utils.asyncio import async_unsafe\n16: from django.utils.dateparse import parse_date, parse_datetime, parse_time\n17: from django.utils.regex_helper import _lazy_re_compile\n18: \n19: from ._functions import register as register_functions\n20: from .client import DatabaseClient\n21: from .creation import DatabaseCreation\n22: from .features import DatabaseFeatures\n23: from .introspection import DatabaseIntrospection\n24: from .operations import DatabaseOperations\n25: from .schema import DatabaseSchemaEditor\n26: \n27: \n28: def decoder(conv_func):\n29:     \"\"\"\n30:     Convert bytestrings from Python's sqlite3 interface to a regular string.\n31:     \"\"\"\n32:     return lambda s: conv_func(s.decode())\n33: \n34: \n35: def adapt_date(val):\n36:     return val.isoformat()\n37: \n38: \n39: def adapt_datetime(val):\n40:     return val.isoformat(\" \")\n41: \n42: \n43: def _get_varchar_column(data):\n44:     if data[\"max_length\"] is None:\n45:         return \"varchar\"\n46:     return \"varchar(%(max_length)s)\" % data\n47: \n48: \n49: Database.register_converter(\"bool\", b\"1\".__eq__)\n50: Database.register_converter(\"date\", decoder(parse_date))\n51: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/sqlite3/base.py%3FL1-50"

Input
{
  "command": "ls -la /workspace/ 2>&1 && find /workspace -name \"*.py\" 2>/dev/null | head -5",
  "description": "List all files in workspace"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "total 0\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 claude claude  26 Mar  9 14:52 .\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 root   root   166 Mar  9 14:52 ..\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 claude claude   6 Mar  9 14:55 .git"
}
117. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace log --all --oneline 2>&1; git -C /workspace remote -v 2>&1; git -C /workspace config --list 2>&1 | head -20",
  "description": "Check git configuration and history"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "core.repositoryformatversion=0\ncore.filemode=true\ncore.bare=false\ncore.logallrefupdates=true\nuser.email=agent@example.com\nuser.name=Agent"
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"259: \\n260: class ConnectionHealthChecksTests(SimpleTestCase):\\n261:     databases = {\\\"default\\\"}\\n262: \\n263:     def setUp(self):\\n264:         # All test cases here need newly configured and created connections.\\n265:         # Use the default db connection for convenience.\\n266:         connection.close()\\n267:         self.addCleanup(connection.close)\\n268: \\n269:     def patch_settings_dict(self, conn_health_checks):\\n270:         self.settings_dict_patcher = patch.dict(\\n271:             connection.settings_dict,\\n272:             {\\n273:                 **connection.settings_dict,\\n274:                 \\\"CONN_MAX_AGE\\\": None,\\n275:                 \\\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\\\": conn_health_checks,\\n276:             },\\n277:         )\\n278:         self.settings_dict_patcher.start()\\n279:         self.addCleanup(self.settings_dict_patcher.stop)\\n280: \\n281:     def run_query(self):\\n282:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\\n283:             cursor.execute(\\\"SELECT 42\\\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix)\\n284: \\n285:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n286:     def test_health_checks_enabled(self):\\n287:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\\n288:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n289:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n290:         # the health check.\\n291:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n292:             self.run_query()\\n293: \\n294:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n295:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n296:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n297:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n298: \\n299:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\\n300:         with patch.object(\\n301:             connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", return_value=False\\n302:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\\n303:             self.run_query()\\n304:             new_connection = connection.connection\\n305:             # A new connection is established.\\n306:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\\n307:             # Only one health check per \\\"request\\\" is performed, so the next\\n308:             # query will carry on even if the health check fails. Next query\\n309:             # succeeds because the real connection is healthy and only the\\n310:             # health check failure is mocked.\\n311:             self.run_query()\\n312:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n313:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\\n314: \\n315:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n316:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n317:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\\n318:         # succeeding.\\n319:         self.run_query()\\n320:         self.run_query()\\n321:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n322: \\n323:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n324:     def test_health_checks_enabled_errors_occurred(self):\\n325:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\\n326:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n327:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n328:         # the health check.\\n329:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n330:             self.run_query()\\n331: \\n332:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n333:         # Simulate errors_occurred.\\n334:         connection.errors_occurred = True\\n335:         # Simulate request_started (the connection is healthy).\\n336:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n337:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\\n338:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n339:         # No additional health checks after the one in\\n340:         # close_if_unusable_or_obsolete() are executed during this \\\"request\\\"\\n341:         # when running queries.\\n342:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n343:             self.run_query()\\n344: \\n345:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n346:     def test_health_checks_disabled(self):\\n347:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=False)\\n348:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n349:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n350:         # the health check.\\n351:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n352:             self.run_query()\\n353: \\n354:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n355:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n356:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n357:         # Persistent connections are enabled (connection is not).\\n358:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n359:         # Health checks are not performed.\\n360:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n361:             self.run_query()\\n362:             # Health check wasn't performed and the connection is unchanged.\\n363:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n364:             self.run_query()\\n365:             # The connection is unchanged after the next query either during\\n366:             # the current \\\"request\\\".\\n367:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n368: \\n369:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n370:     def test_set_autocommit_health_checks_enabled(self):\\n371:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\\n372:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n373:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n374:         # the health check.\\n375:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n376:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\\n377:             # a connection.\\n378:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\\n379:             self.run_query()\\n380:             connection.commit()\\n381:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\\n382: \\n383:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n384:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n385:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n386:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\\n387:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n388: \\n389:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\\n390:         with patch.object(\\n391:             connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", return_value=False\\n392:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\\n393:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\\n394:             # a connection.\\n395:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\\n396:             new_connection = connection.connection\\n397:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\\n398:             # Only one health check per \\\"request\\\" is performed, so a query will\\n399:             # carry on even if the health check fails. This query succeeds\\n400:             # because the real connection is healthy and only the health check\\n401:             # failure is mocked.\\n402:             self.run_query()\\n403:             connection.commit()\\n404:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\\n405:             # The connection is unchanged.\\n406:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n407:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\\n408: \\n409:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n410:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n411:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\\n412:         # succeeding.\\n413:         connection.set_autocommit(False)\\n414:         self.run_query()\\n415:         connection.commit()\\n416:         connection.set_autocommit(True)\\n417:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n418: \\n419: \\n420: class MultiDatabaseTests(TestCase):\\n421: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/django/django@e8ab2bb83fc6d3c0f5d998d1a41ebaebacaa1a28/-/blob/tests/backends/base/test_base.py%3FL259-420\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "259: \n260: class ConnectionHealthChecksTests(SimpleTestCase):\n261:     databases = {\"default\"}\n262: \n263:     def setUp(self):\n264:         # All test cases here need newly configured and created connections.\n265:         # Use the default db connection for convenience.\n266:         connection.close()\n267:         self.addCleanup(connection.close)\n268: \n269:     def patch_settings_dict(self, conn_health_checks):\n270:         self.settings_dict_patcher = patch.dict(\n271:             connection.settings_dict,\n272:             {\n273:                 **connection.settings_dict,\n274:                 \"CONN_MAX_AGE\": None,\n275:                 \"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\": conn_health_checks,\n276:             },\n277:         )\n278:         self.settings_dict_patcher.start()\n279:         self.addCleanup(self.settings_dict_patcher.stop)\n280: \n281:     def run_query(self):\n282:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n283:             cursor.execute(\"SELECT 42\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix)\n284: \n285:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n286:     def test_health_checks_enabled(self):\n287:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\n288:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n289:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n290:         # the health check.\n291:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n292:             self.run_query()\n293: \n294:         old_connection = connection.connection\n295:         # Simulate request_finished.\n296:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n297:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n298: \n299:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\n300:         with patch.object(\n301:             connection, \"is_usable\", return_value=False\n302:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\n303:             self.run_query()\n304:             new_connection = connection.connection\n305:             # A new connection is established.\n306:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\n307:             # Only one health check per \"request\" is performed, so the next\n308:             # query will carry on even if the health check fails. Next query\n309:             # succeeds because the real connection is healthy and only the\n310:             # health check failure is mocked.\n311:             self.run_query()\n312:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n313:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\n314: \n315:         # Simulate request_finished.\n316:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n317:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\n318:         # succeeding.\n319:         self.run_query()\n320:         self.run_query()\n321:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n322: \n323:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n324:     def test_health_checks_enabled_errors_occurred(self):\n325:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\n326:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n327:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n328:         # the health check.\n329:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n330:             self.run_query()\n331: \n332:         old_connection = connection.connection\n333:         # Simulate errors_occurred.\n334:         connection.errors_occurred = True\n335:         # Simulate request_started (the connection is healthy).\n336:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n337:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\n338:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n339:         # No additional health checks after the one in\n340:         # close_if_unusable_or_obsolete() are executed during this \"request\"\n341:         # when running queries.\n342:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n343:             self.run_query()\n344: \n345:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n346:     def test_health_checks_disabled(self):\n347:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=False)\n348:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n349:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n350:         # the health check.\n351:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n352:             self.run_query()\n353: \n354:         old_connection = connection.connection\n355:         # Simulate request_finished.\n356:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n357:         # Persistent connections are enabled (connection is not).\n358:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n359:         # Health checks are not performed.\n360:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n361:             self.run_query()\n362:             # Health check wasn't performed and the connection is unchanged.\n363:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n364:             self.run_query()\n365:             # The connection is unchanged after the next query either during\n366:             # the current \"request\".\n367:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n368: \n369:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n370:     def test_set_autocommit_health_checks_enabled(self):\n371:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\n372:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n373:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n374:         # the health check.\n375:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n376:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\n377:             # a connection.\n378:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\n379:             self.run_query()\n380:             connection.commit()\n381:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\n382: \n383:         old_connection = connection.connection\n384:         # Simulate request_finished.\n385:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n386:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\n387:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n388: \n389:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\n390:         with patch.object(\n391:             connection, \"is_usable\", return_value=False\n392:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\n393:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\n394:             # a connection.\n395:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\n396:             new_connection = connection.connection\n397:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\n398:             # Only one health check per \"request\" is performed, so a query will\n399:             # carry on even if the health check fails. This query succeeds\n400:             # because the real connection is healthy and only the health check\n401:             # failure is mocked.\n402:             self.run_query()\n403:             connection.commit()\n404:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\n405:             # The connection is unchanged.\n406:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n407:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\n408: \n409:         # Simulate request_finished.\n410:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n411:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\n412:         # succeeding.\n413:         connection.set_autocommit(False)\n414:         self.run_query()\n415:         connection.commit()\n416:         connection.set_autocommit(True)\n417:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n418: \n419: \n420: class MultiDatabaseTests(TestCase):\n421: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/django/django@e8ab2bb83fc6d3c0f5d998d1a41ebaebacaa1a28/-/blob/tests/backends/base/test_base.py%3FL259-420"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"236:     @async_unsafe\\n237:     def connect(self):\\n238:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Connect to the database. Assume that the connection is closed.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n239:         # Check for invalid configurations.\\n240:         self.check_settings()\\n241:         # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\\n242:         self.in_atomic_block = False\\n243:         self.savepoint_ids = []\\n244:         self.atomic_blocks = []\\n245:         self.needs_rollback = False\\n246:         # Reset parameters defining when to close/health-check the connection.\\n247:         self.health_check_enabled = self.settings_dict[\\\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\\\"]\\n248:         max_age = self.settings_dict[\\\"CONN_MAX_AGE\\\"]\\n249:         self.close_at = None if max_age is None else time.monotonic() + max_age\\n250:         self.closed_in_transaction = False\\n251:         self.errors_occurred = False\\n252:         # New connections are healthy.\\n253:         self.health_check_done = True\\n254:         # Establish the connection\\n255:         conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\\n256:         self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\\n257:         self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\\\"AUTOCOMMIT\\\"])\\n258:         self.init_connection_state()\\n259:         connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\\n260: \\n261:         self.run_on_commit = []\\n262: \\n263:     def check_settings(self):\\n264:         if self.settings_dict[\\\"TIME_ZONE\\\"] is not None and not settings.USE_TZ:\\n265:             raise ImproperlyConfigured(\\n266: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL236-265\"}",

{
