  "path": "tests/backends/base/test_base.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",
  "startLine": 259
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"259: class ConnectionHealthChecksTests(SimpleTestCase):\\n260:     databases = {\\\"default\\\"}\\n261: \\n262:     def setUp(self):\\n263:         # All test cases here need newly configured and created connections.\\n264:         # Use the default db connection for convenience.\\n265:         connection.close()\\n266:         self.addCleanup(connection.close)\\n267: \\n268:     def patch_settings_dict(self, conn_health_checks):\\n269:         self.settings_dict_patcher = patch.dict(\\n270:             connection.settings_dict,\\n271:             {\\n272:                 **connection.settings_dict,\\n273:                 \\\"CONN_MAX_AGE\\\": None,\\n274:                 \\\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\\\": conn_health_checks,\\n275:             },\\n276:         )\\n277:         self.settings_dict_patcher.start()\\n278:         self.addCleanup(self.settings_dict_patcher.stop)\\n279: \\n280:     def run_query(self):\\n281:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\\n282:             cursor.execute(\\\"SELECT 42\\\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix)\\n283: \\n284:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n285:     def test_health_checks_enabled(self):\\n286:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\\n287:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n288:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n289:         # the health check.\\n290:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n291:             self.run_query()\\n292: \\n293:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n294:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n295:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n296:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n297: \\n298:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\\n299:         with patch.object(\\n300:             connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", return_value=False\\n301:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\\n302:             self.run_query()\\n303:             new_connection = connection.connection\\n304:             # A new connection is established.\\n305:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\\n306:             # Only one health check per \\\"request\\\" is performed, so the next\\n307:             # query will carry on even if the health check fails. Next query\\n308:             # succeeds because the real connection is healthy and only the\\n309:             # health check failure is mocked.\\n310:             self.run_query()\\n311:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n312:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\\n313: \\n314:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n315:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n316:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\\n317:         # succeeding.\\n318:         self.run_query()\\n319:         self.run_query()\\n320:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n321: \\n322:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n323:     def test_health_checks_enabled_errors_occurred(self):\\n324:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\\n325:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n326:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n327:         # the health check.\\n328:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n329:             self.run_query()\\n330: \\n331:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n332:         # Simulate errors_occurred.\\n333:         connection.errors_occurred = True\\n334:         # Simulate request_started (the connection is healthy).\\n335:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n336:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\\n337:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n338:         # No additional health checks after the one in\\n339:         # close_if_unusable_or_obsolete() are executed during this \\\"request\\\"\\n340:         # when running queries.\\n341:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n342:             self.run_query()\\n343: \\n344:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n345:     def test_health_checks_disabled(self):\\n346:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=False)\\n347:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n348:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n349:         # the health check.\\n350:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n351:             self.run_query()\\n352: \\n353:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n354:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n355:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n356:         # Persistent connections are enabled (connection is not).\\n357:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n358:         # Health checks are not performed.\\n359:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n360:             self.run_query()\\n361:             # Health check wasn't performed and the connection is unchanged.\\n362:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n363:             self.run_query()\\n364:             # The connection is unchanged after the next query either during\\n365:             # the current \\\"request\\\".\\n366:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n367: \\n368:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\\\")\\n369:     def test_set_autocommit_health_checks_enabled(self):\\n370:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\\n371:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\\n372:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\\n373:         # the health check.\\n374:         with patch.object(connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", side_effect=AssertionError):\\n375:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\\n376:             # a connection.\\n377:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\\n378:             self.run_query()\\n379:             connection.commit()\\n380:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\\n381: \\n382:         old_connection = connection.connection\\n383:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n384:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n385:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\\n386:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\\n387: \\n388:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\\n389:         with patch.object(\\n390:             connection, \\\"is_usable\\\", return_value=False\\n391:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\\n392:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\\n393:             # a connection.\\n394:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\\n395:             new_connection = connection.connection\\n396:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\\n397:             # Only one health check per \\\"request\\\" is performed, so a query will\\n398:             # carry on even if the health check fails. This query succeeds\\n399:             # because the real connection is healthy and only the health check\\n400:             # failure is mocked.\\n401:             self.run_query()\\n402:             connection.commit()\\n403:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\\n404:             # The connection is unchanged.\\n405:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n406:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\\n407: \\n408:         # Simulate request_finished.\\n409:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n410:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\\n411:         # succeeding.\\n412:         connection.set_autocommit(False)\\n413:         self.run_query()\\n414:         connection.commit()\\n415:         connection.set_autocommit(True)\\n416:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\\n417: \\n418: \\n419: class MultiDatabaseTests(TestCase):\\n420:     databases = {\\\"default\\\", \\\"other\\\"}\\n421: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/tests/backends/base/test_base.py%3FL259-420\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "259: class ConnectionHealthChecksTests(SimpleTestCase):\n260:     databases = {\"default\"}\n261: \n262:     def setUp(self):\n263:         # All test cases here need newly configured and created connections.\n264:         # Use the default db connection for convenience.\n265:         connection.close()\n266:         self.addCleanup(connection.close)\n267: \n268:     def patch_settings_dict(self, conn_health_checks):\n269:         self.settings_dict_patcher = patch.dict(\n270:             connection.settings_dict,\n271:             {\n272:                 **connection.settings_dict,\n273:                 \"CONN_MAX_AGE\": None,\n274:                 \"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\": conn_health_checks,\n275:             },\n276:         )\n277:         self.settings_dict_patcher.start()\n278:         self.addCleanup(self.settings_dict_patcher.stop)\n279: \n280:     def run_query(self):\n281:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n282:             cursor.execute(\"SELECT 42\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix)\n283: \n284:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n285:     def test_health_checks_enabled(self):\n286:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\n287:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n288:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n289:         # the health check.\n290:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n291:             self.run_query()\n292: \n293:         old_connection = connection.connection\n294:         # Simulate request_finished.\n295:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n296:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n297: \n298:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\n299:         with patch.object(\n300:             connection, \"is_usable\", return_value=False\n301:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\n302:             self.run_query()\n303:             new_connection = connection.connection\n304:             # A new connection is established.\n305:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\n306:             # Only one health check per \"request\" is performed, so the next\n307:             # query will carry on even if the health check fails. Next query\n308:             # succeeds because the real connection is healthy and only the\n309:             # health check failure is mocked.\n310:             self.run_query()\n311:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n312:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\n313: \n314:         # Simulate request_finished.\n315:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n316:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\n317:         # succeeding.\n318:         self.run_query()\n319:         self.run_query()\n320:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n321: \n322:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n323:     def test_health_checks_enabled_errors_occurred(self):\n324:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\n325:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n326:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n327:         # the health check.\n328:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n329:             self.run_query()\n330: \n331:         old_connection = connection.connection\n332:         # Simulate errors_occurred.\n333:         connection.errors_occurred = True\n334:         # Simulate request_started (the connection is healthy).\n335:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n336:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\n337:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n338:         # No additional health checks after the one in\n339:         # close_if_unusable_or_obsolete() are executed during this \"request\"\n340:         # when running queries.\n341:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n342:             self.run_query()\n343: \n344:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n345:     def test_health_checks_disabled(self):\n346:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=False)\n347:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n348:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n349:         # the health check.\n350:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n351:             self.run_query()\n352: \n353:         old_connection = connection.connection\n354:         # Simulate request_finished.\n355:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n356:         # Persistent connections are enabled (connection is not).\n357:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n358:         # Health checks are not performed.\n359:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n360:             self.run_query()\n361:             # Health check wasn't performed and the connection is unchanged.\n362:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n363:             self.run_query()\n364:             # The connection is unchanged after the next query either during\n365:             # the current \"request\".\n366:             self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n367: \n368:     @skipUnlessDBFeature(\"test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n369:     def test_set_autocommit_health_checks_enabled(self):\n370:         self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=True)\n371:         self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)\n372:         # Newly created connections are considered healthy without performing\n373:         # the health check.\n374:         with patch.object(connection, \"is_usable\", side_effect=AssertionError):\n375:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\n376:             # a connection.\n377:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\n378:             self.run_query()\n379:             connection.commit()\n380:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\n381: \n382:         old_connection = connection.connection\n383:         # Simulate request_finished.\n384:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n385:         # Persistent connections are enabled.\n386:         self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)\n387: \n388:         # Simulate connection health check failing.\n389:         with patch.object(\n390:             connection, \"is_usable\", return_value=False\n391:         ) as mocked_is_usable:\n392:             # Simulate outermost atomic block: changing autocommit for\n393:             # a connection.\n394:             connection.set_autocommit(False)\n395:             new_connection = connection.connection\n396:             self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)\n397:             # Only one health check per \"request\" is performed, so a query will\n398:             # carry on even if the health check fails. This query succeeds\n399:             # because the real connection is healthy and only the health check\n400:             # failure is mocked.\n401:             self.run_query()\n402:             connection.commit()\n403:             connection.set_autocommit(True)\n404:             # The connection is unchanged.\n405:             self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n406:         self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)\n407: \n408:         # Simulate request_finished.\n409:         connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n410:         # The underlying connection is being reused further with health checks\n411:         # succeeding.\n412:         connection.set_autocommit(False)\n413:         self.run_query()\n414:         connection.commit()\n415:         connection.set_autocommit(True)\n416:         self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)\n417: \n418: \n419: class MultiDatabaseTests(TestCase):\n420:     databases = {\"default\", \"other\"}\n421: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/tests/backends/base/test_base.py%3FL259-420"
  }
}

        "file": "tests/servers/tests.py",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c/-/blob/tests/servers/tests.py"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "34: the database in each HTTP request. They're controlled by the\n35: :setting:`CONN_MAX_AGE` parameter which defines the maximum lifetime of a\n36: connection. It can be set independently for each database.\n37: ",
            "endLine": 37,
            "startLine": 34
          },
          {
            "content": "39: database connection at the end of each request. To enable persistent\n40: connections, set :setting:`CONN_MAX_AGE` to a positive integer of seconds. For\n41: unlimited persistent connections, set it to ``None``.\n42: ",
            "endLine": 42,
            "startLine": 39
          },
          {
            "content": "52: Django closes the connection once it exceeds the maximum age defined by\n53: :setting:`CONN_MAX_AGE` or when it isn't usable any longer.\n54: \n55: ",
            "endLine": 55,
            "startLine": 52
          }
        ],

  "content": "{\"content\":\"60:         self.settings_dict = settings_dict\\n61:         self.alias = alias\\n62:         # Query logging in debug mode or when explicitly enabled.\\n63:         self.queries_log = deque(maxlen=self.queries_limit)\\n64:         self.force_debug_cursor = False\\n65: \\n66:         # Transaction related attributes.\\n67:         # Tracks if the connection is in autocommit mode. Per PEP 249, by\\n68:         # default, it isn't.\\n69:         self.autocommit = False\\n70:         # Tracks if the connection is in a transaction managed by 'atomic'.\\n71:         self.in_atomic_block = False\\n72:         # Increment to generate unique savepoint ids.\\n73:         self.savepoint_state = 0\\n74:         # List of savepoints created by 'atomic'.\\n75:         self.savepoint_ids = []\\n76:         # Stack of active 'atomic' blocks.\\n77:         self.atomic_blocks = []\\n78:         # Tracks if the outermost 'atomic' block should commit on exit,\\n79:         # ie. if autocommit was active on entry.\\n80:         self.commit_on_exit = True\\n81:         # Tracks if the transaction should be rolled back to the next\\n82:         # available savepoint because of an exception in an inner block.\\n83:         self.needs_rollback = False\\n84:         self.rollback_exc = None\\n85: \\n86:         # Connection termination related attributes.\\n87:         self.close_at = None\\n88:         self.closed_in_transaction = False\\n89:         self.errors_occurred = False\\n90:         self.health_check_enabled = False\\n91:         self.health_check_done = False\\n92: \\n93:         # Thread-safety related attributes.\\n94:         self._thread_sharing_lock = threading.Lock()\\n95:         self._thread_sharing_count = 0\\n96:         self._thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\\n97: \\n98:         # A list of no-argument functions to run when the transaction commits.\\n99:         # Each entry is an (sids, func, robust) tuple, where sids is a set of\\n100:         # the active savepoint IDs when this function was registered and robust\\n101:         # specifies whether it's allowed for the function to fail.\\n102:         self.run_on_commit = []\\n103: \\n104:         # Should we run the on-commit hooks the next time set_autocommit(True)\\n105:         # is called?\\n106:         self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = False\\n107: \\n108:         # A stack of wrappers to be invoked around execute()/executemany()\\n109:         # calls. Each entry is a function taking five arguments: execute, sql,\\n110:         # params, many, and context. It's the function's responsibility to\\n111:         # call execute(sql, params, many, context).\\n112:         self.execute_wrappers = []\\n113: \\n114:         self.client = self.client_class(self)\\n115:         self.creation = self.creation_class(self)\\n116:         self.features = self.features_class(self)\\n117:         self.introspection = self.introspection_class(self)\\n118:         self.ops = self.ops_class(self)\\n119:         self.validation = self.validation_class(self)\\n120: \\n121: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL60-120\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "60:         self.settings_dict = settings_dict\n61:         self.alias = alias\n62:         # Query logging in debug mode or when explicitly enabled.\n63:         self.queries_log = deque(maxlen=self.queries_limit)\n64:         self.force_debug_cursor = False\n65: \n66:         # Transaction related attributes.\n67:         # Tracks if the connection is in autocommit mode. Per PEP 249, by\n68:         # default, it isn't.\n69:         self.autocommit = False\n70:         # Tracks if the connection is in a transaction managed by 'atomic'.\n71:         self.in_atomic_block = False\n72:         # Increment to generate unique savepoint ids.\n73:         self.savepoint_state = 0\n74:         # List of savepoints created by 'atomic'.\n75:         self.savepoint_ids = []\n76:         # Stack of active 'atomic' blocks.\n77:         self.atomic_blocks = []\n78:         # Tracks if the outermost 'atomic' block should commit on exit,\n79:         # ie. if autocommit was active on entry.\n80:         self.commit_on_exit = True\n81:         # Tracks if the transaction should be rolled back to the next\n82:         # available savepoint because of an exception in an inner block.\n83:         self.needs_rollback = False\n84:         self.rollback_exc = None\n85: \n86:         # Connection termination related attributes.\n87:         self.close_at = None\n88:         self.closed_in_transaction = False\n89:         self.errors_occurred = False\n90:         self.health_check_enabled = False\n91:         self.health_check_done = False\n92: \n93:         # Thread-safety related attributes.\n94:         self._thread_sharing_lock = threading.Lock()\n95:         self._thread_sharing_count = 0\n96:         self._thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n97: \n98:         # A list of no-argument functions to run when the transaction commits.\n99:         # Each entry is an (sids, func, robust) tuple, where sids is a set of\n100:         # the active savepoint IDs when this function was registered and robust\n101:         # specifies whether it's allowed for the function to fail.\n102:         self.run_on_commit = []\n103: \n104:         # Should we run the on-commit hooks the next time set_autocommit(True)\n105:         # is called?\n106:         self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = False\n107: \n108:         # A stack of wrappers to be invoked around execute()/executemany()\n109:         # calls. Each entry is a function taking five arguments: execute, sql,\n110:         # params, many, and context. It's the function's responsibility to\n111:         # call execute(sql, params, many, context).\n112:         self.execute_wrappers = []\n113: \n114:         self.client = self.client_class(self)\n115:         self.creation = self.creation_class(self)\n116:         self.features = self.features_class(self)\n117:         self.introspection = self.introspection_class(self)\n118:         self.ops = self.ops_class(self)\n119:         self.validation = self.validation_class(self)\n120: \n121: ",

Prefer connections['default'] instead.\\n43: connection = ConnectionProxy(connections, DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS)\\n44: \\n45: \\n46: # Register an event to reset saved queries when a Django request is started.\\n47: def reset_queries(**kwargs):\\n48:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\\n49:         conn.queries_log.clear()\\n50: \\n51: \\n52: signals.request_started.connect(reset_queries)\\n53: \\n54: \\n55: # Register an event to reset transaction state and close connections past\\n56: # their lifetime.\\n57: def close_old_connections(**kwargs):\\n58:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\\n59:         conn.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\\n60: \\n61: \\n62: signals.request_started.connect(close_old_connections)\\n63: signals.request_finished.connect(close_old_connections)\\n64: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/__init__.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: from django.core import signals\n2: from django.db.utils import (\n3:     DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS,\n4:     DJANGO_VERSION_PICKLE_KEY,\n5:     ConnectionHandler,\n6:     ConnectionRouter,\n7:     DatabaseError,\n8:     DataError,\n9:     Error,\n10:     IntegrityError,\n11:     InterfaceError,\n12:     InternalError,\n13:     NotSupportedError,\n14:     OperationalError,\n15:     ProgrammingError,\n16: )\n17: from django.utils.connection import ConnectionProxy\n18: \n19: __all__ = [\n20:     \"close_old_connections\",\n21:     \"connection\",\n22:     \"connections\",\n23:     \"reset_queries\",\n24:     \"router\",\n25:     \"DatabaseError\",\n26:     \"IntegrityError\",\n27:     \"InternalError\",\n28:     \"ProgrammingError\",\n29:     \"DataError\",\n30:     \"NotSupportedError\",\n31:     \"Error\",\n32:     \"InterfaceError\",\n33:     \"OperationalError\",\n34:     \"DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS\",\n35:     \"DJANGO_VERSION_PICKLE_KEY\",\n36: ]\n37: \n38: connections = ConnectionHandler()\n39: \n40: router = ConnectionRouter()\n41: \n42: # For backwards compatibility. Prefer connections['default'] instead.\n43: connection = ConnectionProxy(connections, DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS)\n44: \n45: \n46: # Register an event to reset saved queries when a Django request is started.\n47: def reset_queries(**kwargs):\n48:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\n49:         conn.queries_log.clear()\n50: \n51: \n52: signals.request_started.connect(reset_queries)\n53: \n54: \n55: # Register an event to reset transaction state and close connections past\n56: # their lifetime.\n57: def close_old_connections(**kwargs):\n58:     for conn in connections.all(initialized_only=True):\n59:         conn.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()\n60: \n61: \n62: signals.request_started.connect(close_old_connections)\n63: signals.request_finished.connect(close_old_connections)\n64: ",

132. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 310,

  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",
  "startLine": 270
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"270:     @async_unsafe\\n271:     def ensure_connection(self):\\n272:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Guarantee that a connection to the database is established.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n273:         if self.connection is None:\\n274:             if self.in_atomic_block and self.closed_in_transaction:\\n275:                 raise ProgrammingError(\\n276:                     \\\"Cannot open a new connection in an atomic block.\\\"\\n277:                 )\\n278:             with self.wrap_database_errors:\\n279:                 self.connect()\\n280: \\n281:     # ##### Backend-specific wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\\n282: \\n283:     def _prepare_cursor(self, cursor):\\n284:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n285:         Validate the connection is usable and perform database cursor wrapping.\\n286:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n287:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\\n288:         if self.queries_logged:\\n289:             wrapped_cursor = self.make_debug_cursor(cursor)\\n290:         else:\\n291:             wrapped_cursor = self.make_cursor(cursor)\\n292:         return wrapped_cursor\\n293: \\n294:     def _cursor(self, name=None):\\n295:         self.close_if_health_check_failed()\\n296:         self.ensure_connection()\\n297:         with self.wrap_database_errors:\\n298:             return self._prepare_cursor(self.create_cursor(name))\\n299: \\n300:     def _commit(self):\\n301:         if self.connection is not None:\\n302:             with debug_transaction(self, \\\"COMMIT\\\"), self.wrap_database_errors:\\n303:                 return self.connection.commit()\\n304: \\n305:     def _rollback(self):\\n306:         if self.connection is not None:\\n307:             with debug_transaction(self, \\\"ROLLBACK\\\"), self.wrap_database_errors:\\n308:                 return self.connection.rollback()\\n309: \\n310:     def _close(self):\\n311: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL270-310\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "270:     @async_unsafe\n271:     def ensure_connection(self):\n272:         \"\"\"Guarantee that a connection to the database is established.\"\"\"\n273:         if self.connection is None:\n274:             if self.in_atomic_block and self.closed_in_transaction:\n275:                 raise ProgrammingError(\n276:                     \"Cannot open a new connection in an atomic block.\"\n277:                 )\n278:             with self.wrap_database_errors:\n279:                 self.connect()\n280: \n281:     # ##### Backend-specific wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\n282: \n283:     def _prepare_cursor(self, cursor):\n284:         \"\"\"\n285:         Validate the connection is usable and perform database cursor wrapping.\n286:         \"\"\"\n287:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\n288:         if self.queries_logged:\n289:             wrapped_cursor = self.make_debug_cursor(cursor)\n290:         else:\n291:             wrapped_cursor = self.make_cursor(cursor)\n292:         return wrapped_cursor\n293: \n294:     def _cursor(self, name=None):\n295:         self.close_if_health_check_failed()\n296:         self.ensure_connection()\n297:         with self.wrap_database_errors:\n298:             return self._prepare_cursor(self.create_cursor(name))\n299: \n300:     def _commit(self):\n301:         if self.connection is not None:\n302:             with debug_transaction(self, \"COMMIT\"), self.wrap_database_errors:\n303:                 return self.connection.commit()\n304: \n305:     def _rollback(self):\n306:         if self.connection is not None:\n307:             with debug_transaction(self, \"ROLLBACK\"), self.wrap_database_errors:\n308:                 return self.connection.rollback()\n309: \n310:     def _close(self):\n311: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL270-310"
  }
}

cd0479ff764272add5e0aba2afcf5649a241ca00.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c\",\"title\":\"Fixed #36468 -- Fixed failure to close popup when adding a related object in the <mlissner@michaeljaylissner.com>\",\"commit\":\"4cecf30\",\"date\":\"2026-01-15\",\"message\":\"Fixed #36865 -- Removed casting from exact lookups in admin searches.\\n\\nInstead of casting non-text fields to CharField (which prevents index\\nusage), skip exact lookups when the search term fails\\nformfield.to_python().\\n\\nThis preserves index usage for valid searches while gracefully handling\\ninvalid search terms by simply not including them in the query for that\\nfield.\\n\\nFor multi-term searches like 'foo 123' on search_fields=['name', 'age__exact']:\\n- 'foo': invalid for age, so only name lookup is used\\n- '123': valid for both, so both lookups are used\\n\\nThis entails a slight increase in permissive... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c\",\"title\":\"Fixed #36865 -- Removed casting from exact lookups in admin searches.\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c/-/commit/4cecf3039586ea738afafb9a28c946bff42c37c1\"},{\"author\":\"mlissner <mlissner@michaeljaylissner.com>\",\"commit\":\"b25bc24\",\"date\":\"2026-01-30\",\"message\":\"Refs #36865 -- Added test for invalid value handling in admin changelist.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c\",\"title\":\"Refs #36865 -- Added test for invalid value handling in admin changelist.\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c/-/commit/b25bc2441827a31b5629e9f79ad7296b992648a2\"},{\"author\":\"Natalia <124304+nessita@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"f87c205\",\"date\":\"2026-01-29\",\"message\":\"Fixed spell checking in docs GitHub Actions workflow.\\n\\nThe spelling check job was passing even with spelling errors because\\nthe system spell checker (`aspell`) was not installed on the GitHub\\nActions runner. While `sphinxcontrib.spelling` and `PyEnchant` were\\ninstalled via pip, they require a system-level spell checker backend\\nto function.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c\",\"title\":\"Fixed spell checking in docs GitHub Actions workflow.\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c/-/commit/f87c2055b45356378a7c2a020eb872352d20f85e\"},{\"author\":\"Natalia <124304+nessita@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"117ff1d\",\"date\":\"2026-01-29\",\"message\":\"Made explicit that aspell is a requirement to run spell checks on docs.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c\",\"title\":\"Made explicit that aspell is a requirement to run spell checks on docs.\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c/-/commit/117ff1d37d63cdfa0982eba6b636bf8f59d99a3c\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit repo:github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c count:10\",\"totalCount\":10}",

        "message": "Fixed #36468 -- Fixed failure to close popup when adding a related object in the admin.\n\nThe issue manifested when there were multiple relations and only some\nof them participated in a filter_horizontal.\n\nRegression in cd0479ff764272add5e0aba2afcf5649a241ca00.",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",

        "author": "mlissner <mlissner@michaeljaylissner.com>",
        "commit": "4cecf30",
        "date": "2026-01-15",
        "message": "Fixed #36865 -- Removed casting from exact lookups in admin searches.\n\nInstead of casting non-text fields to CharField (which prevents index\nusage), skip exact lookups when the search term fails\nformfield.to_python().\n\nThis preserves index usage for valid searches while gracefully handling\ninvalid search terms by simply not including them in the query for that\nfield.\n\nFor multi-term searches like 'foo 123' on search_fields=['name', 'age__exact']:\n- 'foo': invalid for age, so only name lookup is used\n- '123': valid for both, so both lookups are used\n\nThis entails a slight increase in permissive...         "title": "Fixed #36865 -- Removed casting from exact lookups in admin searches.",

        "commit": "b25bc24",

        "message": "Refs #36865 -- Added test for invalid value handling in admin changelist.",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",
        "title": "Refs #36865 -- Added test for invalid value handling in admin changelist.",

      },
      {
        "author": "Natalia <124304+nessita@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "f87c205",
        "date": "2026-01-29",
        "message": "Fixed spell checking in docs GitHub Actions workflow.\n\nThe spelling check job was passing even with spelling errors because\nthe system spell checker (`aspell`) was not installed on the GitHub\nActions runner. While `sphinxcontrib.spelling` and `PyEnchant` were\ninstalled via pip, they require a system-level spell checker backend\nto function.",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",
        "title": "Fixed spell checking in docs GitHub Actions workflow.",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c/-/commit/f87c2055b45356378a7c2a020eb872352d20f85e"
      },
      {
        "author": "Natalia <124304+nessita@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "117ff1d",
        "date": "2026-01-29",
        "message": "Made explicit that aspell is a requirement to run spell checks on docs.",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",
        "title": "Made explicit that aspell is a requirement to run spell checks on docs.",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c/-/commit/117ff1d37d63cdfa0982eba6b636bf8f59d99a3c"

  "content": "{\"content\":\"440:     # ##### Backend-specific transaction management methods #####\\n441: \\n442:     def _set_autocommit(self, autocommit):\\n443:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n444:         Backend-specific implementation to enable or disable autocommit.\\n445:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n446:         raise NotImplementedError(\\n447:             \\\"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a _set_autocommit() method\\\"\\n448:         )\\n449: \\n450:     # ##### Generic transaction management methods #####\\n451: \\n452:     def get_autocommit(self):\\n453:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the autocommit state.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n454:         self.ensure_connection()\\n455:         return self.autocommit\\n456: \\n457:     def set_autocommit(\\n458:         self, autocommit, force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=False\\n459:     ):\\n460:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n461:         Enable or disable autocommit.\\n462: \\n463:         The usual way to start a transaction is to turn autocommit off.\\n464:         SQLite does not properly start a transaction when disabling\\n465:         autocommit. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\\n466:         transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required. Using\\n467:         force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True will issue an\\n468:         explicit BEGIN with SQLite. This option will be ignored for other\\n469:         backends.\\n470:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n471:         self.validate_no_atomic_block()\\n472:         self.close_if_health_check_failed()\\n473: \\\"BEGIN\\\"):\\n487:                 self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\\n488:         self.autocommit = autocommit\\n489: \\n490:         if autocommit and self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on:\\n491:             self.run_and_clear_commit_hooks()\\n492:             self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = False\\n493: \\n494:     def  def set_rollback(self, rollback):\\n503:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n504:         Set or unset the \\\"needs rollback\\\" flag -- for *advanced use* only.\\n505:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n506:         if not   "content": "{\"content\":\"320:         return self._cursor()\\n321: \\n322:     @async_unsafe\\n323:     def commit(self):\\n324:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Commit a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n325:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\\n326:         self.validate_no_atomic_block()\\n327:         self._commit()\\n328:         # A successful commit means that the database connection works.\\n329:         self.errors_occurred = False\\n330:         self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = True\\n331: \\n332:     @async_unsafe\\n333:     def rollback(self):\\n334:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Roll back a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n335:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\\n336:         self.validate_no_atomic_block()\\n337:         self._rollback()\\n338:         # A successful rollback means that the database connection works.\\n339:         self.errors_occurred = False\\n340:         self.needs_rollback = False\\n341:         self.run_on_commit = []\\n342: \\n343:     @async_unsafe\\n344:     def close(self):\\n345:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Close the connection to the database.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n346:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\\n347:         self.run_on_commit = []\\n348: \\n349:         # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\\n350:         # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\\n351:         # will reset the transaction state anyway.\\n352:         if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\\n353:             return\\n354:         try:\\n355:             self._close()\\n356:         finally:\\n357:             if self.in_atomic_block:\\n358:                 self.closed_in_transaction = True\\n359:                 self.needs_rollback = True\\n360:             else:\\n361:                 self.connection = None\\n362: \\n363:     # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\\n364: \\n365:     def _savepoint(self, sid):\\n366:         with self.cursor() as cursor:\\n367:             cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\\n368: \\n369:     def _savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\\n370:         with self.cursor() as cursor:\\n371:             cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_rollback_sql(sid))\\n372: \\n373:     def _savepoint_commit(self, sid):\\n374:         with self.cursor() as cursor:\\n375:             cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_commit_sql(sid))\\n376: \\n377:     def _savepoint_allowed(self):\\n378:         # Savepoints cannot be created outside a transaction\\n379:         return self.features.uses_savepoints and not self.get_autocommit()\\n380: \\n381:     # ##### Generic savepoint management methods #####\\n382: \\n383:     @async_unsafe\\n384:     def savepoint(self):\\n385:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n386:         Create a savepoint inside the current transaction. Return an\\n387:         identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\\n388:         rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\\n389:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n390:         if not self._savepoint_allowed():\\n391:             return\\n392: \\n393:         thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\\n394:         tid = str(thread_ident).replace(\\\"-\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n395: \\n396:         self.savepoint_state += 1\\n397:         sid = \\\"s%s_x%d\\\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\\n398: \\n399:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\\n400:         self._savepoint(sid)\\n401: \\n402:         return sid\\n403: \\n404:     @async_unsafe\\n405:     def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\\n406:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n407:         Roll back to a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\\n408:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n409:         if not self._savepoint_allowed():\\n410:             return\\n411: \\n412:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\\n413:         self._savepoint_rollback(sid)\\n414: \\n415:         # Remove any callbacks registered while this savepoint was active.\\n416:         self.run_on_commit = [\\n417:             (sids, func, robust)\\n418:             for (sids, func, robust) in self.run_on_commit\\n419:             if sid not in sids\\n420:         ]\\n421: \\n422:     @async_unsafe\\n423:     def savepoint_commit(self, sid):\\n424:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n425:         Release a savepoint. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\\n426:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n427:         if not self._savepoint_allowed():\\n428:             return\\n429: \\n430:         self.validate_thread_sharing()\\n431:         self._savepoint_commit(sid)\\n432: \\n433:     @async_unsafe\\n434:     def clean_savepoints(self):\\n435:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n436:         Reset the counter used to generate unique savepoint ids in this thread.\\n437:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n438:         self.savepoint_state = 0\\n439: \\n440:     # ##### Backend-specific transaction management methods #####\\n441: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/backends/base/base.py%3FL320-440\"}",

142. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 200,
  "path": "django/db/utils.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"140: \\n141: class ConnectionHandler(BaseConnectionHandler):\\n142:     settings_name = \\\"DATABASES\\\"\\n143:     # Connections needs to still be an actual thread local, as it's truly\\n144:     # thread-critical. Database backends should use @async_unsafe to protect\\n145:     # their code from async contexts, but this will give those contexts\\n146:     # separate connections in case it's needed as well. There's no cleanup\\n147:     # after async contexts, though, so we don't allow that if we can help it.\\n148:     thread_critical = True\\n149: \\n150:     def configure_settings(self, databases):\\n151:         databases = super().configure_settings(databases)\\n152:         if databases == {}:\\n153:             databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] = {\\\"ENGINE\\\": \\\"django.db.backends.dummy\\\"}\\n154:         elif DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS not in databases:\\n155:             raise ImproperlyConfigured(\\n156:                 f\\\"You must define a '{DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}' database.\\\"\\n157:             )\\n158:         elif databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] == {}:\\n159:             databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS][\\\"ENGINE\\\"] = \\\"django.db.backends.dummy\\\"\\n160: \\n161:         # Configure default settings.\\n162:         for conn in databases.values():\\n163:             conn.setdefault(\\\"ATOMIC_REQUESTS\\\", False)\\n164:             conn.setdefault(\\\"AUTOCOMMIT\\\", True)\\n165:             conn.setdefault(\\\"ENGINE\\\", \\\"django.db.backends.dummy\\\")\\n166:             if conn[\\\"ENGINE\\\"] == \\\"django.db.backends.\\\" or not conn[\\\"ENGINE\\\"]:\\n167:                 conn[\\\"ENGINE\\\"] = \\\"django.db.backends.dummy\\\"\\n168:             conn.setdefault(\\\"CONN_MAX_AGE\\\", 0)\\n169:             conn.setdefault(\\\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\\\", False)\\n170:             conn.setdefault(\\\"OPTIONS\\\", {})\\n171:             conn.setdefault(\\\"TIME_ZONE\\\", None)\\n172:             for setting in [\\\"NAME\\\", \\\"USER\\\", \\\"PASSWORD\\\", \\\"HOST\\\", \\\"PORT\\\"]:\\n173:                 conn.setdefault(setting, \\\"\\\")\\n174: \\n175:             test_settings = conn.setdefault(\\\"TEST\\\", {})\\n176:             default_test_settings = [\\n177:                 (\\\"CHARSET\\\", None),\\n178:                 (\\\"COLLATION\\\", None),\\n179:                 (\\\"MIGRATE\\\", True),\\n180:                 (\\\"MIRROR\\\", None),\\n181:                 (\\\"NAME\\\", None),\\n182:             ]\\n183:             for key, value in default_test_settings:\\n184:                 test_settings.setdefault(key, value)\\n185:         return databases\\n186: \\n187:     @property\\n188:     def databases(self):\\n189:         # Maintained for backward compatibility as some 3rd party packages have\\n190:         # made use of this private API in the past. It is no longer used within\\n191:         # Django itself.\\n192:         return self.settings\\n193: \\n194:     def create_connection(self, alias):\\n195:         db = self.settings[alias]\\n196:         backend = load_backend(db[\\\"ENGINE\\\"])\\n197:         return backend.DatabaseWrapper(db, alias)\\n198: \\n199: \\n200: class ConnectionRouter:\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/db/utils.py%3FL140-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "140: \n141: class ConnectionHandler(BaseConnectionHandler):\n142:     settings_name = \"DATABASES\"\n143:     # Connections needs to still be an actual thread local, as it's truly\n144:     # thread-critical. Database backends should use @async_unsafe to protect\n145:     # their code from async contexts, but this will give those contexts\n146:     # separate connections in case it's needed as well. There's no cleanup\n147:     # after async contexts, though, so we don't allow that if we can help it.\n148:     thread_critical = True\n149: \n150:     def configure_settings(self, databases):\n151:         databases = super().configure_settings(databases)\n152:         if databases == {}:\n153:             databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] = {\"ENGINE\": \"django.db.backends.dummy\"}\n154:         elif DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS not in databases:\n155:             raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n156:                 f\"You must define a '{DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}' database.\"\n157:             )\n158:         elif databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS] == {}:\n159:             databases[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS][\"ENGINE\"] = \"django.db.backends.dummy\"\n160: \n161:         # Configure default settings.\n162:         for conn in databases.values():\n163:             conn.setdefault(\"ATOMIC_REQUESTS\", False)\n164:             conn.setdefault(\"AUTOCOMMIT\", True)\n165:             conn.setdefault(\"ENGINE\", \"django.db.backends.dummy\")\n166:             if conn[\"ENGINE\"] == \"django.db.backends.\" or not conn[\"ENGINE\"]:\n167:                 conn[\"ENGINE\"] = \"django.db.backends.dummy\"\n168:             conn.setdefault(\"CONN_MAX_AGE\", 0)\n169:             conn.setdefault(\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\", False)\n170:             conn.setdefault(\"OPTIONS\", {})\n171:             conn.setdefault(\"TIME_ZONE\", None)\n172:             for setting in [\"NAME\", \"USER\", \"PASSWORD\", \"HOST\", \"PORT\"]:\n173:                 conn.setdefault(setting, \"\")\n174: \n175:             test_settings = conn.setdefault(\"TEST\", {})\n176:             default_test_settings = [\n177:                 (\"CHARSET\", None),\n178:                 (\"COLLATION\", None),\n179:                 (\"MIGRATE\", True),\n180:                 (\"MIRROR\", None),\n181:                 (\"NAME\", None),\n182:             ]\n183:             for key, value in default_test_settings:\n184:                 test_settings.setdefault(key, value)\n185:         return databases\n186: \n187:     @property\n188:     def databases(self):\n189:         # Maintained for backward compatibility as some 3rd party packages have\n190:         # made use of this private API in the past. It is no longer used within\n191:         # Django itself.\n192:         return self.settings\n193: \n194:     def create_connection(self, alias):\n195:         db = self.settings[alias]\n196:         backend = load_backend(db[\"ENGINE\"])\n197:         return backend.DatabaseWrapper(db, alias)\n198: \n199: \n200: class ConnectionRouter:\n201: ",

  "stdout": "            \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require an is_usable() method\"\n        )\n\n    def close_if_health_check_failed(self):\n        \"\"\"Close existing connection if it fails a health check.\"\"\"\n        if (\n            self.connection is None\n            or not self.health_check_enabled\n            or self.health_check_done\n        ):\n            return\n\n        if not self.is_usable():\n            self.close()\n        self.health_check_done = True\n\n    def close_if_unusable_or_obsolete(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Close the current connection if unrecoverable errors have occurred\n        or if it outlived its maximum age.\n        \"\"\""
}

146. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "sed -n '236,270p' /home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/django/db/backends/base/base.py",
  "description": "Read full connect() method from installed Django 5.2"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    @async_unsafe\n    def connect(self):\n        \"\"\"Connect to the database. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n        # Check for invalid configurations.\n        self.check_settings()\n        # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n        self.in_atomic_block = False\n        self.savepoint_ids = []\n        self.atomic_blocks = []\n        self.needs_rollback = False\n        # Reset parameters defining when to close/health-check the connection.\n        self.health_check_enabled = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_HEALTH_CHECKS\"]\n        max_age = self.settings_dict[\"CONN_MAX_AGE\"]\n        self.close_at = None if max_age is None else time.monotonic() + max_age\n        self.closed_in_transaction = False\n        self.errors_occurred = False\n        # New connections are healthy.\n        self.health_check_done = True\n        # Establish the connection\n        conn_params = self.get_connection_params()\n        self.connection = self.get_new_connection(conn_params)\n        self.set_autocommit(self.settings_dict[\"AUTOCOMMIT\"])\n        self.init_connection_state()\n        connection_created.send(sender=self.__class__, connection=self)\n\n        self.run_on_commit = []\n\n    def check_settings(self):\n        if self.settings_dict[\"TIME_ZONE\"] is not None and not settings.USE_TZ:\n            raise ImproperlyConfigured(\n                \"Connection '%s' cannot set TIME_ZONE because USE_TZ is False.\"\n                % self.alias\n            )\n\n    @async_unsafe"
}

django.db.backends.base.base import BaseDatabaseWrapper\\n6: from django.test import (\\n7:     SimpleTestCase,\\n8:     TestCase,\\n9:     TransactionTestCase,\\n10:     skipUnlessDBFeature,\\n11: )\\n12: from django.test.runner import DebugSQLTextTestResult\\n13: from django.test.utils import CaptureQueriesContext, override_settings\\n14: \\n15: from ..models import Person, Square\\n16: \\n17: \\n18: class DatabaseWrapperTests(SimpleTestCase):\\n19:     def test_repr(self):\\n20:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\\n21:         self.assertEqual(\\n22:             repr(conn),\\n23:             f\\\"<DatabaseWrapper vendor={connection.vendor!r} alias='default'>\\\",\\n24:         )\\n25: \\n26:     def test_initialization_class_attributes(self):\\n27:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n28:         The \\\"initialization\\\" class attributes like client_class and\\n29:         creation_class should be set on the class and reflected in the\\n30:         corresponding instance attributes of the instantiated backend.\\n31:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n32:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\\n33:         conn_class = type(conn)\\n34:         attr_names = [\\n35:             (\\\"client_class\\\", \\\"client\\\"),\\n36:             (\\\"creation_class\\\", \\\"creation\\\"),\\n37:             (\\\"features_class\\\", \\\"features\\\"),\\n38:             (\\\"introspection_class\\\", \\\"introspection\\\"),\\n39:             (\\\"ops_class\\\", \\\"ops\\\"),\\n40:             (\\\"validation_class\\\", \\\"validation\\\"),\\n41:         ]\\n42:         for class_attr_name, instance_attr_name in attr_names:\\n43:             class_attr_value = getattr(conn_class, class_attr_name)\\n44:             self.assertIsNotNone(class_attr_value)\\n45:             instance_attr_value = getattr(conn, instance_attr_name)\\n46:             self.assertIsInstance(instance_attr_value, class_attr_value)\\n47: \\n48:     def test_initialization_display_name(self):\\n49:         self.assertEqual(BaseDatabaseWrapper.display_name, \\\"unknown\\\")\\n50:         self.assertNotEqual(connection.display_name, \\\"unknown\\\")\\n51: \\n52:     def test_get_database_version(self):\\n53:         with patch.object(BaseDatabaseWrapper, \\\"__init__\\\", return_value=None):\\n54:             msg = (\\n55:                 \\\"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a \\\"\\n56:                 \\\"get_database_version() method.\\\"\\n57:             )\\n58:             with self.assertRaisesMessage(NotImplementedError, msg):\\n59:                 BaseDatabaseWrapper().get_database_version()\\n60: \\n61:     def test_check_database_version_supported_with_none_as_database_version(self):\\n62:         with patch.object(connection.features, \\\"minimum_database_version\\\", None):\\n63:             connection.check_database_version_supported()\\n64: \\n65:     def test_release_memory_without_garbage_collection(self):\\n66:         # Schedule the restore of the garbage collection settings.\\n67:         self.addCleanup(gc.set_debug, 0)\\n68:         self.addCleanup(gc.enable)\\n69: \\n70:         # Disable automatic garbage collection to control when it's triggered,\\n71:         # then run a full collection cycle to ensure `gc.garbage` is empty.\\n72:         gc.disable()\\n73:         gc.collect()\\n74: \\n75:         # The garbage list isn't automatically populated to avoid CPU overhead,\\n76:         # so debugging needs to be enabled to track all unreachable items and\\n77:         # have them stored in `gc.garbage`.\\n78:         gc.set_debug(gc.DEBUG_SAVEALL)\\n79: \\n80:         # Create a new connection that will be closed during the test, and also\\n81:         # ensure that a `DatabaseErrorWrapper` is created for this connection.\\n82:         test_connection = connection.copy()\\n83:         with test_connection.wrap_database_errors:\\n84:             self.assertEqual(test_connection.queries, [])\\n85: \\n86:         # Close the connection and remove references to it. This will mark all\\n87:         # objects related to the connection as garbage to be collected.\\n88:         test_connection.close()\\n89:         test_connection = None\\n90: \\n91:         # Enforce garbage collection to populate `gc.garbage` for inspection.\\n92:         gc.collect()\\n93:         self.assertEqual(gc.garbage, [])\\n94: \\n95: \\n96: class DatabaseWrapperLoggingTests(TransactionTestCase):\\n97:     available_apps = [\\\"backends\\\"]\\n98: \\n99:     @override_settings(DEBUG=True)\\n100:     def test_commit_debug_log(self):\\n101:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\\n102:         with CaptureQueriesContext(conn):\\n103:             with self.assertLogs(\\\"django.db.backends\\\", \\\"DEBUG\\\") as cm:\\n104:                 with transaction.atomic():\\n105:                     Person.objects.create(first_name=\\\"first\\\", last_name=\\\"last\\\")\\n106: \\n107:                 self.assertGreaterEqual(len(conn.queries_log), 3)\\n108:                 self.assertEqual(conn.queries_log[-3][\\\"sql\\\"], \\\"BEGIN\\\")\\n109:                 self.assertRegex(\\n110:                     cm.output[0],\\n111:                     r\\\"DEBUG:django.db.backends:\\\\(\\\\d+.\\\\d{3}\\\\) \\\"\\n112:                     rf\\\"BEGIN; args=None; alias={DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}\\\",\\n113:                 )\\n114:                 self.assertEqual(conn.queries_log[-1][\\\"sql\\\"], \\\"COMMIT\\\")\\n115:                 self.assertRegex(\\n116:                     cm.output[-1],\\n117:                     r\\\"DEBUG:django.db.backends:\\\\(\\\\d+.\\\\d{3}\\\\) \\\"\\n118:                     rf\\\"COMMIT; args=None; alias={DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}\\\",\\n119:                 )\\n120: \\n121:     @override_settings(DEBUG=True)\\n122:     def test_rollback_debug_log(self):\\n123:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\\n124:         with CaptureQueriesContext(conn):\\n125:             with self.assertLogs(\\\"django.db.backends\\\", \\\"DEBUG\\\") as cm:\\n126:                 with self.assertRaises(Exception), transaction.atomic():\\n127:                     Person.objects.create(first_name=\\\"first\\\", last_name=\\\"last\\\")\\n128:                     raise Exception(\\\"Force rollback\\\")\\n129: \\n130:                 self.assertEqual(conn.queries_log[-1][\\\"sql\\\"], \\\"ROLLBACK\\\")\\n131:                 self.assertRegex(\\n132:                     cm.output[-1],\\n133:                     r\\\"DEBUG:django.db.backends:\\\\(\\\\d+.\\\\d{3}\\\\) \\\"\\n134:                     rf\\\"ROLLBACK; args=None; alias={DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}\\\",\\n135:                 )\\n136: \\n137:     def test_no_logs_without_debug(self):\\n138:         if isinstance(self._outcome.result, DebugSQLTextTestResult):\\n139:             self.skipTest(\\\"--debug-sql interferes with this test\\\")\\n140:         with self.assertNoLogs(\\\"django.db.backends\\\", \\\"DEBUG\\\"):\\n141:             with self.assertRaises(Exception), transaction.atomic():\\n142:                 Person.objects.create(first_name=\\\"first\\\", last_name=\\\"last\\\")\\n143:                 raise Exception(\\\"Force rollback\\\")\\n144: \\n145:             conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\\n146:             self.assertEqual(len(conn.queries_log), 0)\\n147: \\n148: \\n149: class ExecuteWrapperTests(TestCase):\\n150:     @staticmethod\\n151:     def call_execute(connection, params=None):\\n152:         ret_val = \\\"1\\\" if params is None else \\\"%s\\\"\\n153:         sql = \\\"SELECT \\\" + ret_val + connection.features.bare_select_suffix\\n154:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\\n155:             cursor.execute(sql, params)\\n156: \\n157:     def call_executemany(self, connection, params=None):\\n158:         # executemany() must use an update query. Make sure it does nothing\\n159:         # by putting a false condition in the WHERE clause.\\n160:         sql = \\\"DELETE FROM {} WHERE 0=1 AND 0=%s\\\".format(Square._meta.db_table)\\n161:         if params is None:\\n162:             params = [(i,) for i in range(3)]\\n163:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\\n164:             cursor.executemany(sql, params)\\n165: \\n166:     @staticmethod\\n167:     def mock_wrapper():\\n168:         return MagicMock(side_effect=lambda execute, *args: execute(*args))\\n169: \\n170:     def test_wrapper_invoked(self):\\n171: connection.execute_wrapper(wrapper):\\n184:             self.call_executemany(connection)\\n185:         self.assertTrue(wrapper.called)\\n186:         (_, sql, param_list, many, context), _ = self.assertEqual(context[\\\"connection\\\"], connection)\\n191: \\n192:     def test_database_queried(self):\\n193:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\\n194:         with connection.execute_wrapper(wrapper):\\n195:             with connection.cursor() as cursor:\\n196:                 sql = \\\"SELECT 17\\\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix\\n197:                 cursor.execute(sql)\\n198:                 seventeen = cursor.fetchall()\\n199:                 self.assertEqual(list(seventeen), [(17,)])\\n200:             self.call_executemany(connection)\\n201: \\n202:     def test_nested_wrapper_invoked(self):\\n203:         outer_wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\\n204:         inner_wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\\n205:         with (\\n206:             connection.execute_wrapper(outer_wrapper),\\n207:             connection.execute_wrapper(inner_wrapper),\\n208:         ):\\n209:             self.call_execute(connection)\\n210:             self.assertEqual(inner_wrapper.call_count, 1)\\n211:             self.call_executemany(connection)\\n212:             self.assertEqual(inner_wrapper.call_count, 2)\\n213: \\n214:     def test_outer_wrapper_blocks(self):\\n215:         def blocker(*args):\\n216:             pass\\n217: \\n218:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\\n219:         c = connection  # This alias shortens the next line.\\n220:         with (\\n221:             c.execute_wrapper(wrapper),\\n222:             c.execute_wrapper(blocker),\\n223:             c.execute_wrapper(wrapper),\\n224:         ):\\n225:             with c.cursor() as cursor:\\n226:                 cursor.execute(\\\"The database never sees this\\\")\\n227:                 self.assertEqual(wrapper.call_count, 1)\\n228:                 cursor.executemany(\\\"The database never sees this %s\\\", [(\\\"either\\\",)])\\n229:                 self.assertEqual(wrapper.call_count, 2)\\n230: \\n231:     def test_wrapper_gets_sql(self):\\n232:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\\n233:         sql = \\\"SELECT 'aloha'\\\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix\\n234:         with connection.execute_wrapper(wrapper), connection.cursor() as cursor:\\n235:             cursor.execute(sql)\\n236:         (_, reported_sql, _, _, _), _ = wrapper.call_args\\n237:         self.assertEqual(reported_sql, sql)\\n238: \\n239:     def test_wrapper_connection_specific(self):\\n240:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\\n241:         with connections[\\\"other\\\"].execute_wrapper(wrapper):\\n242:             self.assertEqual(connections[\\\"other\\\"].execute_wrappers, [wrapper])\\n243:             self.call_execute(connection)\\n244:         self.assertFalse(wrapper.called)\\n245:         self.assertEqual(connection.execute_wrappers, [])\\n246:         self.assertEqual(connections[\\\"other\\\"].execute_wrappers, [])\\n247: \\n248:     def test_wrapper_debug(self):\\n249:         def wrap_with_comment(execute, sql, params, many, context):\\n250:             return execute(f\\\"/* My comment */ {sql}\\\", params, many, context)\\n251: \\n252:         with CaptureQueriesContext(connection) as ctx:\\n253:             with connection.execute_wrapper(wrap_with_comment):\\n254: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/tests/backends/base/test_base.py%3FL1-260\"}",

    "content": "1: import gc\n2: from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch\n3: \n4: from django.db import DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS, connection, connections, transaction\n5: from django.db.backends.base.base import BaseDatabaseWrapper\n6: from django.test import (\n7:     SimpleTestCase,\n8:     TestCase,\n9:     TransactionTestCase,\n10:     skipUnlessDBFeature,\n11: )\n12: from django.test.runner import DebugSQLTextTestResult\n13: from django.test.utils import CaptureQueriesContext, override_settings\n14: \n15: from ..models import Person, Square\n16: \n17: \n18: class DatabaseWrapperTests(SimpleTestCase):\n19:     def test_repr(self):\n20:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\n21:         self.assertEqual(\n22:             repr(conn),\n23:             f\"<DatabaseWrapper vendor={connection.vendor!r} alias='default'>\",\n24:         )\n25: \n26:     def test_initialization_class_attributes(self):\n27:         \"\"\"\n28:         The \"initialization\" class attributes like client_class and\n29:         creation_class should be set on the class and reflected in the\n30:         corresponding instance attributes of the instantiated backend.\n31:         \"\"\"\n32:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\n33:         conn_class = type(conn)\n34:         attr_names = [\n35:             (\"client_class\", \"client\"),\n36:             (\"creation_class\", \"creation\"),\n37:             (\"features_class\", \"features\"),\n38:             (\"introspection_class\", \"introspection\"),\n39:             (\"ops_class\", \"ops\"),\n40:             (\"validation_class\", \"validation\"),\n41:         ]\n42:         for class_attr_name, instance_attr_name in attr_names:\n43:             class_attr_value = getattr(conn_class, class_attr_name)\n44:             self.assertIsNotNone(class_attr_value)\n45:             instance_attr_value = getattr(conn, instance_attr_name)\n46:             self.assertIsInstance(instance_attr_value, class_attr_value)\n47: \n48:     def test_initialization_display_name(self):\n49:         self.assertEqual(BaseDatabaseWrapper.display_name, \"unknown\")\n50:         self.assertNotEqual(connection.display_name, \"unknown\")\n51: \n52:     def test_get_database_version(self):\n53:         with patch.object(BaseDatabaseWrapper, \"__init__\", return_value=None):\n54:             msg = (\n55:                 \"subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a \"\n56:                 \"get_database_version() method.\"\n57:             )\n58:             with self.assertRaisesMessage(NotImplementedError, msg):\n59:                 BaseDatabaseWrapper().get_database_version()\n60: \n61:     def test_check_database_version_supported_with_none_as_database_version(self):\n62:         with patch.object(connection.features, \"minimum_database_version\", None):\n63:             connection.check_database_version_supported()\n64: \n65:     def test_release_memory_without_garbage_collection(self):\n66:         # Schedule the restore of the garbage collection settings.\n67:         self.addCleanup(gc.set_debug, 0)\n68:         self.addCleanup(gc.enable)\n69: \n70:         # Disable automatic garbage collection to control when it's triggered,\n71:         # then run a full collection cycle to ensure `gc.garbage` is empty.\n72:         gc.disable()\n73:         gc.collect()\n74: \n75:         # The garbage list isn't automatically populated to avoid CPU overhead,\n76:         # so debugging needs to be enabled to track all unreachable items and\n77:         # have them stored in `gc.garbage`.\n78:         gc.set_debug(gc.DEBUG_SAVEALL)\n79: \n80:         # Create a new connection that will be closed during the test, and also\n81:         # ensure that a `DatabaseErrorWrapper` is created for this connection.\n82:         test_connection = connection.copy()\n83:         with test_connection.wrap_database_errors:\n84:             self.assertEqual(test_connection.queries, [])\n85: \n86:         # Close the connection and remove references to it. This will mark all\n87:         # objects related to the connection as garbage to be collected.\n88:         test_connection.close()\n89:         test_connection = None\n90: \n91:         # Enforce garbage collection to populate `gc.garbage` for inspection.\n92:         gc.collect()\n93:         self.assertEqual(gc.garbage, [])\n94: \n95: \n96: class DatabaseWrapperLoggingTests(TransactionTestCase):\n97:     available_apps = [\"backends\"]\n98: \n99:     @override_settings(DEBUG=True)\n100:     def test_commit_debug_log(self):\n101:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\n102:         with CaptureQueriesContext(conn):\n103:             with self.assertLogs(\"django.db.backends\", \"DEBUG\") as cm:\n104:                 with transaction.atomic():\n105:                     Person.objects.create(first_name=\"first\", last_name=\"last\")\n106: \n107:                 self.assertGreaterEqual(len(conn.queries_log), 3)\n108:                 self.assertEqual(conn.queries_log[-3][\"sql\"], \"BEGIN\")\n109:                 self.assertRegex(\n110:                     cm.output[0],\n111:                     r\"DEBUG:django.db.backends:\\(\\d+.\\d{3}\\) \"\n112:                     rf\"BEGIN; args=None; alias={DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}\",\n113:                 )\n114:                 self.assertEqual(conn.queries_log[-1][\"sql\"], \"COMMIT\")\n115:                 self.assertRegex(\n116:                     cm.output[-1],\n117:                     r\"DEBUG:django.db.backends:\\(\\d+.\\d{3}\\) \"\n118:                     rf\"COMMIT; args=None; alias={DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}\",\n119:                 )\n120: \n121:     @override_settings(DEBUG=True)\n122:     def test_rollback_debug_log(self):\n123:         conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\n124:         with CaptureQueriesContext(conn):\n125:             with self.assertLogs(\"django.db.backends\", \"DEBUG\") as cm:\n126:                 with self.assertRaises(Exception), transaction.atomic():\n127:                     Person.objects.create(first_name=\"first\", last_name=\"last\")\n128:                     raise Exception(\"Force rollback\")\n129: \n130:                 self.assertEqual(conn.queries_log[-1][\"sql\"], \"ROLLBACK\")\n131:                 self.assertRegex(\n132:                     cm.output[-1],\n133:                     r\"DEBUG:django.db.backends:\\(\\d+.\\d{3}\\) \"\n134:                     rf\"ROLLBACK; args=None; alias={DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS}\",\n135:                 )\n136: \n137:     def test_no_logs_without_debug(self):\n138:         if isinstance(self._outcome.result, DebugSQLTextTestResult):\n139:             self.skipTest(\"--debug-sql interferes with this test\")\n140:         with self.assertNoLogs(\"django.db.backends\", \"DEBUG\"):\n141:             with self.assertRaises(Exception), transaction.atomic():\n142:                 Person.objects.create(first_name=\"first\", last_name=\"last\")\n143:                 raise Exception(\"Force rollback\")\n144: \n145:             conn = connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS]\n146:             self.assertEqual(len(conn.queries_log), 0)\n147: \n148: \n149: class ExecuteWrapperTests(TestCase):\n150:     @staticmethod\n151:     def call_execute(connection, params=None):\n152:         ret_val = \"1\" if params is None else \"%s\"\n153:         sql = \"SELECT \" + ret_val + connection.features.bare_select_suffix\n154:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n155:             cursor.execute(sql, params)\n156: \n157:     def call_executemany(self, connection, params=None):\n158:         # executemany() must use an update query. Make sure it does nothing\n159:         # by putting a false condition in the WHERE clause.\n160:         sql = \"DELETE FROM {} WHERE 0=1 AND 0=%s\".format(Square._meta.db_table)\n161:         if params is None:\n162:             params = [(i,) for i in range(3)]\n163:         with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n164:             cursor.executemany(sql, params)\n165: \n166:     @staticmethod\n167:     def mock_wrapper():\n168:         return MagicMock(side_effect=lambda execute, *args: execute(*args))\n169: \n170:     def test_wrapper_invoked(self):\n171:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n172:         with connection.execute_wrapper(wrapper):\n173:             self.call_execute(connection)\n174:         self.assertTrue(wrapper.called)\n175:         (_, sql, params, many, context), _ = wrapper.call_args\n176:         self.assertIn(\"SELECT\", sql)\n177:         self.assertIsNone(params)\n178:         self.assertIs(many, False)\n179:         self.assertEqual(context[\"connection\"], connection)\n180: \n181:     def test_wrapper_invoked_many(self):\n182:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n183:         with connection.execute_wrapper(wrapper):\n184:             self.call_executemany(connection)\n185:         self.assertTrue(wrapper.called)\n186:         (_, sql, param_list, many, context), _ = wrapper.call_args\n187:         self.assertIn(\"DELETE\", sql)\n188:         self.assertIsInstance(param_list, (list, tuple))\n189:         self.assertIs(many, True)\n190:         self.assertEqual(context[\"connection\"], connection)\n191: \n192:     def test_database_queried(self):\n193:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n194:         with connection.execute_wrapper(wrapper):\n195:             with connection.cursor() as cursor:\n196:                 sql = \"SELECT 17\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix\n197:                 cursor.execute(sql)\n198:                 seventeen = cursor.fetchall()\n199:                 self.assertEqual(list(seventeen), [(17,)])\n200:             self.call_executemany(connection)\n201: \n202:     def test_nested_wrapper_invoked(self):\n203:         outer_wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n204:         inner_wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n205:         with (\n206:             connection.execute_wrapper(outer_wrapper),\n207:             connection.execute_wrapper(inner_wrapper),\n208:         ):\n209:             self.call_execute(connection)\n210:             self.assertEqual(inner_wrapper.call_count, 1)\n211:             self.call_executemany(connection)\n212:             self.assertEqual(inner_wrapper.call_count, 2)\n213: \n214:     def test_outer_wrapper_blocks(self):\n215:         def blocker(*args):\n216:             pass\n217: \n218:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n219:         c = connection  # This alias shortens the next line.\n220:         with (\n221:             c.execute_wrapper(wrapper),\n222:             c.execute_wrapper(blocker),\n223:             c.execute_wrapper(wrapper),\n224:         ):\n225:             with c.cursor() as cursor:\n226:                 cursor.execute(\"The database never sees this\")\n227:                 self.assertEqual(wrapper.call_count, 1)\n228:                 cursor.executemany(\"The database never sees this %s\", [(\"either\",)])\n229:                 self.assertEqual(wrapper.call_count, 2)\n230: \n231:     def test_wrapper_gets_sql(self):\n232:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n233:         sql = \"SELECT 'aloha'\" + connection.features.bare_select_suffix\n234:         with connection.execute_wrapper(wrapper), connection.cursor() as cursor:\n235:             cursor.execute(sql)\n236:         (_, reported_sql, _, _, _), _ = wrapper.call_args\n237:         self.assertEqual(reported_sql, sql)\n238: \n239:     def test_wrapper_connection_specific(self):\n240:         wrapper = self.mock_wrapper()\n241:         with connections[\"other\"].execute_wrapper(wrapper):\n242:             self.assertEqual(connections[\"other\"].execute_wrappers, [wrapper])\n243:             self.call_execute(connection)\n244:         self.assertFalse(wrapper.called)\n245:         self.assertEqual(connection.execute_wrappers, [])\n246:         self.assertEqual(connections[\"other\"].execute_wrappers, [])\n247: \n248:     def test_wrapper_debug(self):\n249:         def wrap_with_comment(execute, sql, params, many, context):\n250:             return execute(f\"/* My comment */ {sql}\", params, many, context)\n251: \n252:         with CaptureQueriesContext(connection) as ctx:\n253:             with connection.execute_wrapper(wrap_with_comment):\n254:                 list(Person.objects.all())\n255:         last_query = ctx.captured_queries[-1][\"sql\"]\n256:         self.assertTrue(last_query.startswith(\"/* My comment */\"))\n257: \n258: \n259: class ConnectionHealthChecksTests(SimpleTestCase):\n260:     databases = {\"default\"}\n261: ",
